9:50 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update 'Elizabeth Taylor, do you think it's forever?' Bryan West It seems Portofino is always on the singer-songwriter's mind. The Elizabeth Taylor AIDS Foundation shared a congratulatory message on Instagram: "Before MSG hosted this amazing wedding, it hosted Elizabeth Taylor." The post recounted an event on Oct. 17, 1957, when producer Mike Todd threw his wife a lavish celebration for 18,000 guests inside Madison Square Garden, complete with a 14-foot cake, Duke Ellington and Walter Cronkite broadcasting live on CBS. Swift recently paid tribute to the Hollywood icon with an entire song on The Life of a Showgirl and licensed imagery and other intellectual property from Elizabeth Taylor's estate for the music video.In a New York Times interview, Swift explained her deep affection for the late actress: "I love Elizabeth Taylor so much. She fought for artists’ rights. She was exploited in so many ways, and yet she kept her humanity. She kept her humor. She kept her passion for life."

9:37 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update It's been 1,013 days Bryan West Is the clowning ever really over? Is it intentionally masterminded or a cosmic coincidence? July 3, 2026 – the day of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding celebration – comes exactly 1,013 days after Swift made her headline-making first appearance at a Chiefs game on Sept. 24, 2023, when she sat beside Donna Kelce at Arrowhead Stadium as Kansas City defeated the Chicago Bears. 1,013 not only contains her favorite number, 13, but it mirrors the runtime of Swift's longest song, the 10-minute version of "All Too Well" (10:13).

9:14 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update New York got Taylor 'something blue' Liz Schubauer The Empire State Building joined in on the Swift-Kelce wedding festivities by announcing it illuminated its tower in light blue in order to serve as the bride's "something blue." It's the second love story the New York City landmark has participated in this week. On July 1, Russian daredevils climbed to the tip of the building's spire before getting engaged, and then arrested.

8:11 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update It's a rain show Bryan West Moments after Swift and Kelce exchanged vows, rain and flashes of lightning swept across Manhattan outside Madison Square Garden. Swift fans will tell you, especially the ones who've been stuck in open roofed stadiums, that Eras Tour "rain shows" were some of the best. During the final outdoor rain show of the tour in Miami, she called them "very, very special." Rain has a similar reputation in wedding folklore. It symbolizes good fortune, cleansing and a marriage built to last. One popular saying suggests a knot tied on a rainy day is harder to untie. When the rain cleared, a rainbow arched above the New York skyline.

7:52 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Adam Sandler officiates Taylor Swift, Travis wedding Melissa Ruggieri He wasn't there as "The Wedding Singer," but Adam Sandler played a pivotal role in the Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce marriage. Swift's representative, Tree Paine, confirmed that the actor-comedian officiated the ceremony at Madison Square Garden. Swift and Sandler have been friends for the past few years, and the singer has enjoyed a friendship with Sunny and Sadie, Sandler's daughters with wife Jackie. Kelce, meanwhile, is close enough to Sandler that he made a shirtless cameo in last year's "Happy Gilmore 2" as a restaurant manager who fires a busboy (played by Bad Bunny). FULL STORY: Why Adam Sandler officiated the Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce wedding

7:50 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update MSG displays 'JUST&T MARRIED!' About 7:30 p.m. ET, the digital signs outside Madison Square Garden lit up with an announcement: "JUST&T MARRIED!" Word soon spread it was official: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are now husband and wife.

7:45 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Who was Travis Kelce's best man? Wedding party revealed Liz Schubauer A spokesperson for Swift confirmed that the couple did not have bridesmaids or groomsmen. Instead, their brothers were part of the ceremony. Austin Swift served as Taylor's man of honor, while Jason Kelce was Travis' best man.

7:31 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are married! Bryan West Your English teacher and your gym teacher are officially married. 🧨 Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have said "I do" at Madison Square Garden in front of a sea of celebrities, athletes, business moguls, friends and family. FULL STORY: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce marry in Madison Square Garden wedding

6:43 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Silver Chevy SUV believed to be carrying Taylor Swift arrives at MSG Around 6:30 p.m., a silver Chevrolet Suburban SUV departed Taylor Swift's residence and arrived at Madison Square Garden. It's believed the pop star may be inside the vehicle.

6:33 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update No sleep? No problem for the big party Ralphie Aversa There are reports that the Taylor-Travis event at MSG could last until 2 a.m. But like any good party, there might also be an after-party. At least one guest was overheard at a midtown Manhattan hotel explaining his situation: He's anticipating things might not wrap up until much later in the evening, and he has an early flight out of New York City. His solution? Keep the party going until its time to leave for the airport. It could amount to almost a 12-hour fiesta when it's all said and done, which would certainly line up with the moniker "Wedding of the Century." Arrivals were witnessed as early as 2:50 p.m. at the Garden, with a slew of guests leaving their hotels between 3-3:30 p.m.

6:31 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Speak now or forever hold your peace? Ralphie Aversa It's 100 degrees in New York City on the July 4 weekend and 7th Avenue is shut down in front of Penn Station. There was at least one person who thought to protest over the situation. An unidentified woman wearing sunglasses and a face covering made her way down 7th Avenue behind the media pen across from Madison Square Garden. The woman shouted while she held a sign that called Swift "truly a monster" for holding an event at a large venue in Manhattan during the historic heatwave. New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced that cooling centers would be open across the city due to the high temperatures. Earlier in the week, the mayor asked New Yorkers to set their air conditioning to 78 degrees as to not overwhelm the power grid. While in line with recent guidance from the Department of Energy, the recommendation drew the ire of a number of Republicans. Contributing: Amethyst Martinez, USA TODAY NETWORK

6:27 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Karlie Kloss surprises at Taylor Swift wedding Charlie Carballo Karlie Kloss made a gilded arrival. The supermodel, who is a longtime friend to Swift, wore a strapless, liquid-gold satin column gown with a softly draped neckline, accessorizing with a metallic clutch. Her husband, tech entrepreneur Joshua Kushner, complemented the look in a double-breasted tuxedo. Kloss, who recently attended one of Swift's Eras Tour stops in Los Angeles, was also featured in the singer's 2015 "Bad Blood" music video. FULL STORY: Karlie Kloss and Joshua Kushner attend Taylor Swift wedding after fallout rumors

6:03 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Country stars spotted on way to wedding Melissa Ruggieri and Liz Schubauer A gaggle of country music stars was spotted in New York ahead of the wedding. Maren Morris, Kelsea Ballerini, and members of Little Big Town and Sugarland were photographed together. Miranda Lambert and her husband were seen as well.

5:57 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Ed Sheeran shocks in bright blue for Taylor Swift wedding Charlie Carballo Ed Sheeran, who's been rumored to perform at the wedding festivities, made his way to MSG wearing a bright blue suit alongside wife Cherry Seaborn. Full story: Ed Sheeran arrives at Taylor Swift's wedding in bright suit

5:52 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Zoe Kravitz shimmers on the way to Taylor Swift wedding Charlie Carballo Zoë Kravitz wore a sheer, nude-toned cocktail dress adorned with intricate gold and silver beaded floral embroidery, pairing the sparkling look with black accessories and strappy heels.

5:51 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Camila Cabello, Sombr, Benson Boone, more music stars arrive at wedding Charlie Carballo Camila Cabello was pretty in pink, showing a brief glimpse of her gown while making her way to MSG in the back of a car. Benson Boone donned a bowtie with his tux on the way into the Garden. Singer-songwriter Sombr shared a TikTok video where he's seen suiting up and "feeling gorgeous rn" ahead of the wedding. The Chainsmokers duo Alex Pall and Drew Taggart posed for a photo while suited up ahead of the wedding. British singer Ellie Goulding was spotted entering a car while wearing a black cocktail dress.

5:32 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Jason Sudeikis arrives at MSG Liz Schubauer and Charlie Carballo Jason Sudeikis, wearing a tuxedo, made his arrival to the wedding. Kansas City is his hometown, and he's a longtime Chiefs fan.

3:47 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Taylor Swift wedding guests begin arriving at Madison Square Garden Leigh Cuen and Bryan West At least three people are checking iPads for guests lists in front of the arena-turned-wedding venue as more than 50 cars and buses wait to offload guests at Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding celebration. People are showing their phones to security before entering. Actress Dakota Johnson was spotted in a black gown, while Travis' longtime friend Joe Orave posted a photo of his tux via Instagram. Meanwhile, Swift's childhood friend Abigail Anderson was spotted entering a black SUV wearing a dress with a floor-length chocolate brown skirt. “Modern Family” actor Eric Stonestreet was seen making his way to a car with wife Lindsay Schweitzer, who was wearing a shimmery dusty rose metallic evening dress. Tommy Hilfiger and his wife, Dee Ocleppo Hilfiger, posed for a photo in the back of their car, with Ocleppo Hilfiger wearing a vibrant red gown accented by an oversized turquoise shoulder bow and matching floral earrings. Kansas City Chiefs running back Kareem Hunt was seen making his way to the event. "Shark Tank" investor Barbara Corcoran, who has been a longtime Taylor Swift fan, teased that she’s “getting ready for a very special wedding… such a love story.” Roughly 1,000 wedding guests are expected to continue arriving until 5 p.m. ET and may stay to party until the early hours of the morning on Independence Day. Supermodel Gigi Hadid, wearing pink, and among the guests continuing to trickle in, as well as bestselling author Jenny Han and actor Bradley Cooper. Read more

3:25 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Taylor Swift wedding livestream: See outside Madison Square Garden

2:17 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update From cakes to candy, brands embrace the Swift-Kelce wedding hype Caché McClay Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are expected to tie the knot today. So business owners, fans and everyone else in between are trying to get in on the action. From candy makers to bakers and wedding favor brands, people and companies around the world appear to be capitalizing on what could be the biggest wedding of the era. While not yet verified, cake designer Julie Deffense shared what appeared to be the couple's wedding cake on Instagram, saying that it traveled more than 1,200 miles for the highly anticipated event. Deffense operates out of both Portugal and Florida. The caption read: "A very special cake delivery for a very special couple! Can’t wait to share more soon!" Along those same lines, a company named Sticky Lollies claimed to create custom candies for the soon-to-be married couple, sharing photos of the treats on Instagram. "Wedding custom orders are the best! Being apart of peoples special day is so lovely for us. Thank you to Taylor and Travis for their custom order, hope you have a great love story," the company wrote. Meanwhile, in Kansas City, the Instagram account for Missouri's historic landmark that was once a train station also posted about the famous couple. "Tonight, we light up Union Station in orange and teal for Taylor Swift and red and gold for Travis Kelce," the Instagram account, which might typically focus on promoting the station's planetarium and science center, posted on the night of July 2. Businesses and craftsmen around the world are continuing to post congratulations for the soon-to-be newlyweds.

2:07 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Jason and Travis Kelce post special 'New Heights' episode with Prince William Jack McKessy The Kelce brothers released a special episode of their "New Heights" podcast on July 3 featuring Prince William, heir apparent to the British throne, as their guest. While the reported wedding between Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift was never mentioned, Prince William did joke about the Kansas City Chiefs tight end's "most iconic moment at Wembley Stadium" in London. Jason asked the prince which of Travis' most notable performances at Wembley was more iconic: a touchdown he scored against the Lions at the stadium in 2015, or his appearance as a backup dancer for Swift during her Eras Tour's Wembley stop. "Travis as a backup dancer," Prince William answered. "I think you should do it again." During the rest of the episode, most of the talk focused on Prince William's soccer fandom and the English national team's performance at the 2026 World Cup. The Prince of Wales said he's "quietly confident" in this year's squad after their round of 32 win over DR Congo on July 1. He also said he hopes to see the Kelce brothers at the World Cup final if England makes it to the match, which will take place at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey, on July 19.

1:15 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Wedding guests leave with monogrammed gift boxes Leigh Cuen Although both Swift and Kelce have yet to confirm the reports, People and Page Six have reported that guests attended the couple's rehearsal diner on July 2 in New York City. Some of those guests, such as football player Ross Travis, were seen leaving the Madison Square Garden area with monogrammed velvet boxes. The image was shared by the celebrity news aggregation Instagram account @deuxmoi, which credited the pop culture outlet @backgrid_usa with finding the photo. It remains to be seen what the superstar couple is giving their guests as wedding favors.

1:00 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Wedding schedule reports emerge Bryan West The New York Times, CNN and ABC reported the couple are expected to host a rehearsal event July 2, followed by a larger celebration on July 3 that could last until 4 a.m. and host roughly 1,000 guests. Neither Swift nor Kelce has publicly confirmed the plans.

12:53 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Trump's White House shares Taylor Swift-themed post ahead of wedding Jennifer Hassan Amid Swift-Kelce wedding fever, Trump found a (blank?) space to insert himself into the holiday weekend mania, taking to social media and sharing an image of himself at the center of an Eras Tour-themed political poster. "It's been a long time coming…," the official White House account wrote in a post on X Thursday, July 2, the same day stars gathered in Maddison Square Garden for what was reportedly the pre-wedding rehearsal dinner. The White House's choice of caption is a lyric from Swift's track "Miss Americana & the Heartbreak Prince." In the X post, Trump features in the middle of the poster, surrounded by other historic figures including Abraham Lincoln and George Washington. "America's Eras Tour" reads the text underneath the image, in reference to Swift's record-breaking Eras Tour. Full story: Trump jumps on Taylor Swift wedding fever with 'America's Eras Tour'

12:36 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Who is designing Taylor Swift's wedding dress? Morgan Evans While Swift has not revealed who designed her gown – and there may be several – there are several possibilities based on designers she's frequently worn throughout her career. Among the names drawing the most attention: Monse, the New York brand founded by Laura Kim and Fernando Garcia (Swift wore some of their looks when they served as co-creative directors at Oscar de la Renta); Stella McCartney (she's rumored to be attending the wedding); Jonathan Anderson of Christian Dior; Vivienne Westwood, known for romantic corseted bridal gowns; Ralph Lauren, whose striped dress Swift wore in her engagement announcement; Oscar de la Renta, a longtime red-carpet favorite; Jackson Wiederhoeft, whose designs Swift has recently embraced; and Givenchy under creative director Sarah Burton. All our predictions: Who's designing Taylor Swift's wedding dress? Here are 5 possibilities

12:19 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Donna Kelce arrives in New York City: Report Anthony Robledo Donna Kelce, the mother of the NFL star, has arrived in New York City ahead of the rumored ceremony, People reported. Swift's soon-to-be mother-in-law, 73, arrived in the city on Thursday, July 2, amid reports that a wedding rehearsal dinner would occur later that night, according to People. The "Traitors" star is also the mother of former Philadelphia Eagles center and "New Heights" podcaster Jason Kelce, 38. On June 24, Jason told reporters he was "Obviously very excited, very excited for my brother. Obviously, a big moment for him. " "I'm very happy for both of them and looking forward to celebrating with them. It's a good year right now, and it's a good year for both of them as well," he said.

12:09 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update A red, white and Taylor Swift weekend Melissa Ruggieri Hardly anyone is talking about the Great American State Fair, unless it's about the light crowds, short lines and acres of open space. But nearly everyone is talking about Taylor Swift and the "wedding of the century." The Fourth of July weekend is the platform for patriotic pride, and this year, Miss Americana will get the headlines, the photo spreads, the last word in her skillfully planned celebration of love. She'll also get to celebrate one of her favorite holidays, as documented through the years from her Rhode Island mansion, in the grandest way possible. Swift is no fan of President Donald Trump, which she confirmed by her endorsement of Kamala Harris during the 2024 election, writing in a post that she was voting for a leader who projects "calm and not chaos." No one except for Swift and Kelce and their inner circle knows if the – again, purported – nuptials were inked for the same weekend as the 250th anniversary of America as a deliberate jab at the administration or if the dates just happened to coincide. But whether this Swift-apalooza was slated to intentionally grab headlines away from Trump blustering through a weekend of red, white and blue-themed activities or is merely an amusingly timed coincidence, there is no doubt the Commander in Chief will spew a late-night word jumble toward the woman who dared steal his spotlight. Swift will either ignore the noise as usual, or smile at the absurdity as a secure, proud childless cat lady. Read more

11:47 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Why is Taylor Swift getting married in New York? Bryan West Few venues in North America offer what Madison Square Garden already has built in: security. Andrea Davis, president and CEO of The Resiliency Initiative, has spent 25 years planning emergency response and crisis management for organizations including Disney, Walmart, the FIFA World Cup and Times Square's New Year's Eve celebration. "Madison Square Garden is a venue known for their security protocol," Davis says. "It's top-notch. When I heard about it being an inside venue – and especially being at Madison – I thought, 'Oh, that just takes all of those security issues off the table.'" The arena also already has experienced personnel, and established procedures for protecting high-profile guests and trusted vendors and staff are accustomed to strict nondisclosure agreements.

11:24 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Madison Square Garden and Taylor Swift have history Bryan West Madison Square Garden does hold personal significance for Swift. She headlined her first show there during the "Fearless" era in 2009, returned for the Speak Now Tour, performed at multiple Jingle Ball concerts and celebrated her 30th birthday at the venue in 2019. She was also courtside at the NBA Finals Game 4 in June. And there is a notable gap in MSG's public calendar. The venue's website lists a June 28 concert by Rüfüs Du Sol and a July 7 performance by Bon Jovi, with no public events scheduled in between. But the venue has been known to go dark before on the long July 4th weekend.

11:12 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Wedding venue: Is Taylor Swift really getting married at MSG? Bryan West Reports from TMZ and the New York Post have linked the couple's celebration to Madison Square Garden, though neither Swift nor Kelce has publicly commented. USA TODAY has confirmed that permits related to Madison Square Garden were filed for July 2 through July 4. However, the records do not identify Swift or Kelce and do not reveal if the event is directly connected to the couple. New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani referenced "Taylor Swift's wedding" while discussing public safety planning for the busy Fourth of July weekend. A city official later clarified that the mayor was referring to previously published media reports rather than confirming independent knowledge of the wedding. Mamdani has agreed to overtime shifts as long as 12 hours for the NYPD. The city does face a possible logjam of events, with World Cup games and celebrations for America's 250th anniversary of independence, a Sail250 maritime gathering – and a wedding that could attract hundreds of fans.

11:08 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Saying 'Taylor Swift' 100,000 times Bryan West Outside the Rutherford Bar, just north of Madison Square Garden, pedestrians can hear the same two words over and over: "Taylor Swift. Taylor Swift. Taylor Swift." Jake Epstein, the Knicks fan who went viral during the NBA Finals for saying "Jalen Brunson" 100,000 times, returned Friday with a new goal – say Swift's name 100,000 times while livestreaming the challenge on YouTube. By late morning, he had passed 15,000 repetitions. Epstein said the stunt doubles as a virtual watch party for fans who couldn't make it to New York. He joked he'd immediately stop if Swift's team offered him a wedding invitation.

11:05 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update MSG wedding: Sly Stone did it before Taylor Swift Anika Reed The Swift-Kelce wedding isn't the first at the venue. Funk musician Sly Stone married model Kathy Silva there in 1974 – in front of more than 20,000 people, including paying fans and celebrity guests (Geraldo Rivera ended up as best man). But the big show of love didn't last: The couple broke up just a few months later and officially divorced in 1976. The Unification Church had a mass wedding there in 1982, with 2075 couples participating in the group ceremony – some of whom reportedly only met a few weeks before – and the men wore identical suits and the women identical gowns.

10:44 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update How much is Taylor Swift's wedding going to cost? Caché McClay Speculation is only growing that the NFL star and pop star will get married at Madison Square Garden in New York City over Independence Day weekend. And their Big Apple wedding is also expected to come with a big price tag. Luxury wedding planner Sonal Shah told the New York Post that it would cost the famous couple nearly $2.5 million just to rent out the arena, estimating the wedding could cost up to $20 million in total. As a billionaire, that speculative budget wouldn't even be a fraction of the "Love Story" singer's net worth. Permitting costs in New York City could be a hefty party of the bill. The New York Times reported that a large event with "extensive impact on the surrounding community" that requires street closures could cost as much as $66,000 a day. The New York Police Department will likely be putting in overtime over the holiday weekend, with a hectic schedule of World Cup, July 4 and America 250th celebrations, and the much-anticipated wedding.

10:28 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce donate $26 million to charities ahead of wedding Melissa Ruggieri The generosity of Taylor Swift continues, even as she and Travis Kelce prepare for their weekend nuptials. This week, they donated $26 million to charities across the country, according to a representative for Swift, with all having ties to the couple through location or personal causes. Among the recipients are several food banks in New York, Los Angeles, Rhode Island, Pennsylvania and Missouri, as well as the national organization Feeding America. FULL STORY: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce donate $26 million to charities ahead of wedding

10:12 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Has Taylor Swift been married before? First weddings for pop star and Travis Kelce Leigh Cuen The festivities will celebrate Swift's first marriage, to another first-timer, Travis Kelce. Although Swift dated actor Joe Alwyn for six years, they never got engaged. The proposal story from Swift's song "Champagne Problems" is widely considered to be a fictional tale. There is no evidence she was ever engaged or married before Kelce. Taylor Swift's dating history, from Joe Jonas and John Mayer to Joe Alwyn Likewise, Kelce has never been married before. Although he has also been in a few public relationships, in addition to the 2016 E! reality TV series "Catching Kelce," where he dated Kentucky native Maya Benberry, none of those relationships ended with a proposal or walking down the aisle. Swift will be the football player's first wife. Dating history: A look at Travis Kelce's exes, including Kayla Nicole, Maya Benberry Both of the soon-to-be newlyweds have talked openly about wanting to have kids. During a "New Heights" podcast episode, Kelce previously said that he sees a family reunion that includes kids of his own as "in our future, for sure." Neither bride nor groom have kids from previous relationships out of wedlock.

10:00 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update SUV possibly carrying Taylor Swift arrives at MSG: Report Anthony Robledo A Chevrolet Suburban SUV, which Reuters reported is believed to be transporting Swift, was photographed arriving at Madison Square Garden on July 2. According to Reuters, the same vehicle was also photographed passing by Swift's townhouse in the city.

9:48 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Taylor Swift wedding security ramps up Bryan West Amidst the barricades already set up, additional officers, units and tactical personnel are expected around the venue. During a Fourth of July public safety news conference, New York Police Commissioner Jessica Tisch acknowledged "an event that we are tracking at Madison Square Garden on Friday night" but declined to provide additional details. "The NYPD will of course have a detail in place, but I'm not going to go into more specifics at this time," Tisch said alongside Mayor Zohran Mamdani at 1 Police Plaza.

9:34 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Taylor Swift wedding dress code Anika Reed The dress code will be black tie, with guests encouraged to wear top hats for men and long gloves for women, for a "magical" summer night, a source told USA TODAY. But what will the bride walk down the aisle wearing? Many fans expect a big, perhaps weird, reveal. Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce wedding: New details as big day nears

9:20 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Who is Abigail Anderson? Meet Taylor Swift's lifelong best friend Charlie Carballo Swift and Kelce's rehearsal dinner guest list is expected to include a mix of A-list stars and close friends and family. But one guest, while not a household name, is sure to catch the attention of longtime Swifties: Abigail Anderson. Anderson and Swift have been close friends for more than 20 years, dating back to their days at Hendersonville High School in Tennessee. The pair met in English class on the first day of their freshman year. Their friendship has remained a constant throughout Swift's career. Anderson was famously name-checked in "Fifteen" and appeared in the song's music video from Swift's 2008 album, "Fearless." More recently, she received another mention in "Ruin the Friendship" from 2025's "The Life of a Showgirl." Anderson has also appeared in Swift's "Miss Americana" documentary and in the music videos for "Picture to Burn," "I'm Only Me When I'm With You" and "New Romantics." In 2017, Swift served as one of Anderson's bridesmaids during her first wedding. Anderson has since remarried and currently works as chief of staff for corporate development at payment technology company Celero Commerce. SPOTTED: Who's going to Taylor Swift's wedding? Star's friends seen in New York

9:12 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Taylor Swift guest list: Who's going to the wedding? Bryan West Ed Sheeran, who is a possible performer at the wedding, was spotted in Malta, New York, (about a three-hour drive from New York City) at pizza restaurant BuonaSera on the Lake with musician Aaron Dessner, who is one of Swift's longtime collaborators. A fan shared a photo on X that appears to show Sabrina Carpenter at Emmett's on Grove in New York City. It won't be a far commute for the New York Knicks' starting five Jalen Brunson, Josh Hart, Mikal Bridges, OG Anunoby and Karl-Anthony Towns, who scored invitations, according to Page Six. After all, Madison Square Garden is where they helped the Knicks win the franchise's first NBA championship in 53 years. Knicks starting 5 gets invite to Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding at MSG, per report Very few details about other guests have emerged. Suki Waterhouse, Selena Gomez, the Haim sisters, Zoë Kravitz, Emma Stone, Gigi Hadid, and Jack Antonoff are all likely attendees. Actress Mariska Hargitay, who joined Swift courtside at Madison Square Garden during Game 4 of the NBA Finals, appears to have one notable gap in her Broadway schedule. During her run in "Every Brilliant Thing," July 3 is the only Friday without a scheduled performance. Ellie Goulding was seen later in the afternoon on Friday, July 3, heading into a car while wearing a black cocktail dress.

9:11 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Prince William makes surprise appearance on 'New Heights' The Kelce brothers have a special royal guest joining their podcast before the Swift wedding extravaganza. “Our guest today is the 6 foot 3' Prince from London, England,” Jason, brother of the groom, announced via Instagram on Friday, July 3. The prince is the official royal patron of the Welsh Rugby Union (WRU) and also the Patron of the Football Association (FA), England's governing body for soccer organizations. These three sports experts are likely to discuss the ongoing World Cup and some of the most important international rugby games of the year, also happening in July, the Nations Championship and the World Rugby Nations Cup. There may be more surprises to come in thespecial "New Heights" podcast episode set to release before the Swift-Kelce wedding in New York City.

8:53 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update When are Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce getting married? Bryan West The couple's nuptials reportedly will be a multi-day event; The New York Times suggested a smaller gathering of about 100 people will take place July 2, with a much bigger celebration on July 3. The timing aligns with both of their schedules: Swift has long associated the holiday with gatherings of friends and family at her Rhode Island estate, while Chiefs training camp begins July 29. ESPN's Nate Taylor previously reported Kelce hoped to marry before camp opens.

"We've got a very special guest making their New Heights debut," the post read, alongside an image showing the silhouette of a man between the Kelce brothers. Although probably not in early July, fans may be able to see or hear footage from the wedding itself in future episodes. A sign spotted at Madison Square Garden said that anyone at the venue from June 29 through July 3 must consent to participate in the photography and videography project taking place at the venue. If not for the "New Heights" podcast, then the footage may be used in a future documentary. Variety reported that Disney executives will be at Madison Square Garden during the event on July 3. Disney previously acquired the exclusive streaming rights to both of Taylor Swift's documentaries about her global Eras Tour in 2024 and 2025.

8:21 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update How long have Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce been dating? A relationship timeline Bryan West July 8, 2023 Kelce danced his way into Arrowhead Stadium attending night two of the Eras Tour. No. 87 hoped to give Swift a friendship bracelet with his phone number on it but was unsuccessful. In her 2023 Time Magazine person of the year interview, Swift said: “We started hanging out right after that." Sept. 24, 2023 Swift set social media on fire by accepting the invitation to cheer on the tight end in the Chiefs' game against the Chicago Bears 41-10. "I think some people think that they saw our first date at that game?" Swift said in the Time magazine interview. "We would never be psychotic enough to hard launch a first date. When you say a relationship is public, that means I’m going to see him do what he loves, we’re showing up for each other, other people are there and we don’t care." Jan. 28, 2024 Swift cheered for the Chiefs at the AFC championship between the Chiefs and Ravens. After clinching the title, Swift went down to the field with Kelce's family to share a kiss and congratulate the tight end on making Super Bowl 58. Feb. 9, 2025 Swift headed to her second Super Bowl to watch the Chiefs and Eagles match-up. She wore a crisp white blazer and red "T" jewelry piece as a necklace. The same beaded jewel was around her thigh at the Grammys. Aug. 26, 2025 Thirteen days after their actual proposal, the couple announced their engagement to the world, posting a carousel of photos to the caption: "Your English teacher and your gym teacher are getting married 🧨." Included in the five photos was a close-up shot of the singer's engagement ring created by Kindred Lubeck with Artifex Fine Jewelry.

8:04 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Taylor Swift wedding planner: Who is behind the celebration? Bryan West Page Six linked luxury event planner Mark Seed to the celebration, a choice that would align with the couple's emphasis on privacy and discretion. Seed maintains one of the lowest public profiles in the celebrity-event industry, but he has previously been connected to high-profile weddings involving Jennifer Lawrence, Jack Antonoff and Margaret Qualley. Fans may remember Antonoff and Qualley's 2023 New Jersey wedding because Swift attended.

search_result.title: Andre Leon Talley auction: An inside look at Christie's collection
search_result.text:
Feb. 16, 2023 Updated Feb. 28, 2023, 9:17 a.m. ET

NEW YORK – We stand on the shoulders of giants. André Leon Talley was indeed an influential fashion giant.

While fashion week descended on New York City, memories of the former Vogue creative director, who died Jan. 18, 2022, stood as the tentpole: his impact as an intrepid fashion journalist, advocate and curator with an oversized wealth of knowledge looms large.

At a live auction Wednesday, individual pieces went for tens of thousands of dollars, according to Christie's auction house. "True to form, the estate auction of André Leon Talley was as grand as he. Far exceeding the pre-sale estimates, Christie's raised over $3.5 million," a post on Talley's Instagram account shared.

Before the items were disbursed, the physical remnants of his memory were on display throughout the week in Midtown Manhattan.

Talley's memory was accented further on a national stage during the Super Bowl halftime show as Rihanna, who was close to Talley, wore a comforter-sized red puffer coat from Alaïa, to seemingly pay homage to Talley's Norma Kamali coat of the same hue.

Need a news break? Check out the all new PLAY hub with puzzles, games and more!

The legend's career met at the intersection of fashion, art and Black history, beginning his journey curating The Metropolitan Museum of Art's Costume Institute before working for Andy Warhol's Interview Magazine. Later, he held editorial titles at fashion fixtures including WWD, Ebony Magazine, W Magazine and later as the first and only Black creative director and later editor at large.

Notches in his timeline of legendary cultural contributions are evident in the well-worn and loved items collected from his home in White Plains, New York.

An inside look at some of the nearly 400 pieces Christie's collected reveals the beauty he carefully surrounded himself with in a life punctuated with both glamour and heartache.

Sale of Talley's items will benefit his first fashion influence: the church

Though Talley witnessed fashion wonders of the world, including YSL runways in Paris and the inner workings of designer Karl Lagerfeld's home, his center of fashion was the Black church he attended with his grandmother as a child in North Carolina.

"We marched off into church and I discovered fashion," Talley said during an episode of PBS' "Finding your Roots" that aired in April. "Everyone in the church was a queen."

Talley entered the most exclusive rooms of fashion, but his collections along the way will benefit his beginnings. Auction proceeds will be split among his two centers of faith: His Harlem church home, Abyssinian Baptist, the first African American Baptist church in New York, and his church home where he grew up: Mt. Sinai Missionary Baptist Church in Durham, North Carolina.

"He loved his church, I think he took all the people that he loved at one point," close friend and fashion designer Diane von Furstenberg told Christie's in a video for the in-person exhibit, highlighting Talley's talent and their lasting friendship.

Talley's collection includes caftans, furniture, Louis Vuitton trunks

The luxurious items in Talley's collection – which were split among Christie's, The Savannah College of Art and Design and his estate – included several hand-painted Louis Vuitton trunks, an Hermès bike, and numerous iconic caftans designed by fashion luminaries Tom Ford, Ralph Rucci, Dapper Dan and von Furstenberg. Quotes from the wordsmith and striking photos peppered the Christie's exhibit, on display in the days before the auction and open to the public to peruse.

Among Talley's possessions were items he'd collected from his travels to several corners of the world, including caftans from designer Patience Torlowei, who visited the exhibit to pay tribute and whom he met in Nigeria when he visited for the first time with Naomi Campbell during Arise Fashion Week.

There was no fashion house that went uncollected among Talley's items, which included coats with gilded buttons made by Gianni Versace before his death in 1997, pieces from Chanel, Gucci, couture Dior, Balenciaga and Prada.

Among the luxury was also the most commonplace, including a pair of NAACP T-shirts he wore to the Met Gala with Whoopi Goldberg in 2010 under a Ralph Rucci cape. The simpler clothing items were made more fabulous with bedazzling and customization (his name beaded on the shoulder with black rhinestones) and L.L. Bean bags inscribed with "Talley" and "Vogue." The pair of custom shirts sold for $1,386 and the pair of canvas bags have a current bid of $2,000.

The exhibit prominently displayed his large collection of Louis Vuitton trunks and bags, including one he carried in the first "Sex and the City" movie. A quote over one of the many clusters reads, "I simply NEED a Louis Vuitton oversized trunk to travel with!" Auction organizers noted they found receipts and scarves within pockets and bags as they curated his belongings.

Pieces from his New York home were grouped together in a salon of sorts, reflecting how his eye for design extended to interiors and replicating exactly how the creations lived within his space.

Multiple extra large Hermès Birkin bags, plate sets from Keith Haring, Interview magazine boxes and Manolo Blahnik shoes showed Talley's flair and sartorial sentimentality.

'A generous and loving friend to me': Naomi Campbell, Anna Wintour, more mourn André Leon Talley

Talley's relationships were reflected in art, photos he collected

Among the physical artifacts of fashion came the visual in the collection obtained by Christie's. Talley's possessions included several artworks and photographs, from modern art greats like former boss and friend Andy Warhol and close friends and mentors, including Diana Vreeland and Lagerfeld.

A significant collection of sketches and Lagerfeld-era Chanel, including a Chanel tennis racket still encased in plastic, showcased Talley and Lagerfeld's yearslong complicated friendship.

Another complex relationship in Talley's life, with Vogue eEditor-in-Chief Anna Wintour, was on display with a singular photo of her, the only image of the fashion boss that sales curators found in his home. The portrait of Wintour, taken by Annie Leibovitz in 2015, sold for $25,200.

Yet Vreeland, former Vogue editor-in-chief and consultant at the Metropolitan Museum of Art's Costume Institute, was prominently featured. A large wall with photographs and art depicting her made up a portion of Talley's collection.

7:04 pm ET July 4, 2026 Share this update Will there be post-wedding bash in Rhode Island? Bella DeCrescenzo and Anthony Robledo Now that the wedding is over, Swifties are wondering whether there will be another celebration the day after in Westerly, Rhode Island, where the musician has historically spent the Fourth of July. She has famously hosted an Independence Day bash at her Watch Hill house from 2013 to 2016. She hosted another bash there in 2023, celebrating with close friends Selena Gomez and the Haim sisters, Este, Alana and Danielle, who were all present at the MSG wedding. Nora Alexander, who owns the boutique Noon in Watch Hill, isn't sure whether Swift will host an after-party in Westerly. "I feel like if I got married last night, I probably wouldn't want to have a big party, but I'm not Taylor Swift and maybe she’s ready for another grand party," Alexander tells the Providence Journal, part of the USA TODAY Network. "She's known to have some big bashes up there on the fourth, so we'll see."

6:47 pm ET July 4, 2026 Share this update Jessica Alba, Tom Brady, Hugh Grant spotted leaving early Anthony Robledo Jessica Alba and her boyfriend, Danny Ramirez, were among the guests who spotted leaving the event earlier than others. Tom Brady was seen departing alone in a car around 10 p.m. on July 3, while Hugh Grant and his wife Anna Eberstein left on foot, just as they had entered. Other celebrities stayed late into the night, including Selena Gomez, who left around 1 a.m. Laura Dern, Ethan Hawke, and his wife, Ryan, reportedly left around 2 a.m. It's unclear when Swift and Kelce left the event. CNN reported that Chris Rock and "Good Morning America" anchor George Stephanopoulos were also spotted leaving earlier.

6:21 pm ET July 4, 2026 Share this update Sabrina Carpenter, Tate McRae stepped up to the mic | Exclusive Anthony Robledo You can't have a singer's wedding without some karaoke. McRae has often cited the "Take It Off" singer as an inspiration, both as a writer and a woman in the music industry.

5:27 pm ET July 4, 2026 Share this update Guests played games for chance at prizes Anthony Robledo In "Miss Americana & the Heartbreak Prince," Swift sings, "Play stupid games and win stupid prizes." The prizes at her wedding were far from stupid, as women had the chance to win Chanel or Dior saddle bags while the men entered to win a lavish golf bag, USA TODAY can confirm. To enter the raffle, guests had to participate in various games, including miniature golf, skee-ball and a football ring toss. 4:53 pm ET July 4, 2026 Share this update A closer look at Karlie Kloss's gown Anthony Robledo Karlie Kloss attended the ceremony wearing an elegant, champagne gold gown with a glossy sheen, a photo shared by celebrity makeup artist Soo Park shows. Speculation that the two had a falling out seemed to be confirmed in 2019 when Kloss invited Scooter Braun to her wedding celebration around the same time he purchased Swift’s master recordings, allegedly without her prior knowledge.

4:02 pm ET July 4, 2026 Share this update Selena Gomez glitters in gold Saman Shafiq Selena Gomez was a vision in a sleeveless, embellished gold gown by Oscar de la Renta at the nuptials, with her hair styled in a chic bob. The singer attended her longtime pal's wedding with her husband, Benny Blanco, according to stories shared by Gomez on Instagram. Gomez also chose Oscar de la Renta for the rehearsal dinner, donning a strapless black gown accessorized with Jimmy Choo heels and jewelry from Fernando Jorge, according to Instagram posts from the designer and Gomez's stylist, Erin Walsh. Swift also wore a gold de la Renta dress to Gomez's 2025 wedding to Blanco. Swift and Gomez have been friends for nearly 20 years. The pop singers first connected in 2008 when Swift was romantically linked to Joe Jonas and Gomez to Nick Jonas. They've publicly celebrated their bond over the years, posing on red carpets together, attending each other's tours and cheering for one another at awards shows.

3:37 pm ET July 4, 2026 Share this update Lena Dunham goes pink and green Saman Shafiq Swift's longtime friend Lena Dunham brought out a custom parrot green bow skirt suit by Amber Doyle for the rehearsal dinner and a custom Christian Cowan outfit featuring a long, black skirt and a pink button-down accessorized with a large blue flower for the wedding day, her stylist Talia Cassel shared. Dunham and Swift have been friends for more than a decade, with Dunham referring to the bride “one of my best friends in the world” in an April interview with People magazine. The two have been spotted together at various event including a Kansas City Chiefs game in December 2025, and Dunham joined the Grammy winner onstage at the 1989 World Tour in 2015. Swift was also a bridesmaid at Dunham's wedding to Luis Felber in 2021.

Also a fan of Dior handbags, the singer has often accessorized her street style looks with some of the brand's most iconic styles, from the Saddle Bag to the Lady Dior, famously named after Princess Diana.

The comedian also shared that she helped Kelce get ready for his SNL night, bringing him Uncrustables in the morning to calm his nerves and make him feel at home. “I was very protective," Gardner said. Did the woman who has spent a career immersed in romance with all of its joys and sorrows really exchange vows in what's essentially her workplace? Personal trainer Rob Jordan and his wife Joann Jordan posted a picture of what appeared to be the venue entrance on Instagram. In one photo, the pair, who have shared photos with Swift and Kelce on social media in the past, stood hand-in-hand as they climbed red carpet-lined stairs. "An unforgettable evening in New York celebrating the beginning of a beautiful new chapter for our dear friends, Travis and Taylor. Kelce, meanwhile, is close enough to Sandler that he made a shirtless cameo in last year's "Happy Gilmore 2" as a restaurant manager who fires a busboy (played by Bad Bunny). The team won the Super Bowl in 2018 and lost to Travis' Chiefs in 2023. Meanwhile, Austin Swift shies away from the spotlight, once appearing at a Christmas Day Chiefs game with his sister disguised as Santa. 9:15 am ET July 4, 2026 Share this update Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce officially wed Bryan West Your English teacher and your gym teacher are officially married. 🧨 Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce said "I do" at Madison Square Garden in front of a sea of celebrities, athletes, business moguls, friends and family. 9:05 am ET July 4, 2026 Share this update Tears and laughter flooded the wedding, wedding insider says Melina Khan Emotions were flowing during the couple's nuptials, one attendee told British outlet LBC News outside Madison Square Garden. "They cried and they laughed and they hugged and they danced and they kissed," the guest said while leaving the area, car window rolled down. Many loved ones played a role in this love story. Swift's mother, Andrea Swift, has credited her cousin Robin, a longtime Kansas City Chiefs fan, with helping set up the newlyweds. It was Robin who helped the couple connect in the first place. In an episode of Swift's six-part Eras Tour docuseries released last year on Disney+, the singer's mom laid out Swift and Kelce's relationship origin story for the first time. "I'm looking at the headlines and perusing around what's on the internet and I see that this guy came to your show and he brought a friendship bracelet and wants to meet you," Andrea said. Mama Swift then called her cousin Robin, who she called the "resident expert on Kansas City Chiefs." "And she goes, 'Oh, my God, he's the nicest guy, and you know what, he really loves his mom,'" Andrea said. "And I went 'ding, ding, ding, ding, ding.'" Now, years later, the couple are happily married.

Kloss, 33, is a longtime friend of Swift, but rumors of a falling out started around 2017 and 2018. Then, Kloss was spotted at Swift’s Eras Tour in 2023 and fans wondered about reconciliation. search_result.title: Met Gala 2017: Everything you need to know about fashion's biggest ...
search_result.text:
Maeve McDermott

USATODAY

Updated May 1, 2017, 4:46 p.m. ET

The Met Gala is more than just the fashion world's version of the Academy Awards. The annual fundraiser for the Metropolitan Museum of Art's Costume Institute brings together the biggest names in entertainment, fashion and politics for one night of revelry.

Take the world's biggest celebrities, dress them in couture and stick them in a room together, and sparks are bound to fly. President Trump proposed to Melania during the event in 2004, and Taylor Swift and Tom Hiddleston's romance was borne of their Met Gala dance-off in 2016. Historically, guests are also supposed to dress in the fashions of the event's honored designer, a tradition that often turns controversial.

Before the stars descend on the Metropolitan Museum of Art in NYC on Monday night, learn more about the annual event.

What is the Met Gala?

The Metropolitan Museum of Art Costume Institute Benefit informally called the Met Gala or the Met Ball, is a black-tie fundraiser for the museum's Costume Institute. The event also celebrates the opening of the Costume Institute's annual fashion exhibit.

Need a news break? Check out the all new PLAY hub with puzzles, games and more!

The Costume Institute is housed in the museum's Anna Wintour Costume Center, named after the Vogue editor-in-chief. Wintour has served as the Met Gala co-chair nearly every year since 1995.

This year's theme

Opening this month, the Costume Institute's new exhibit honors the work of Comme des Garçons designer Rei Kawakubo. The Costume Institute's choice of honoree often sets the tone for the Met Gala, with attendees expected to match the exhibit's theme with their own fashion choices for the evening.

This year, party-goers are encouraged to honor the bold, architectural designs of Kawakubo, who is the first living designer to be honored by the Costume Institute since Yves Saint Laurent in 1983.

The exhibition, which runs from May 4 to September 4, will feature 100+ of Kawakubo’s Comme des Garçons womenswear designs, dating back to her first Paris runway show in 1981.

The celebrity hosts

Every year since Anna Wintour's first year as Met Gala chair in 1995, the event has also featured honorary hosts from the worlds of entertainment and fashion.

Along with Wintour, this year's co-chairs are Katy Perry, Pharrell Williams, Tom Brady and Gisele Bündchen, with Kawakubo also serving as honorary chair.

Ahead of the gala, Perry also appeared on the May cover of Vogue, honoring Kawakubo in a dedicated Comme des Garçons spread.

The star-studded guest list

Only Hollywood's biggest award shows boast guest lists that rival that of the invitation-only Met Gala. Wintour has final say over every attendee, and celebrities from A-list entertainers to political heavyweights mingle together at the event.

While 2016's Manus x Machina gala hosted stars including Beyoncé and Jay Z, Taylor Swift, Kanye West and the entire Kardashian clan, a spokesperson for the museum told The New York Times that the guest list for this year's event is expected to be slightly smaller.

Who is Rei Kawakubo?

Kawakubo, a 74-year-old Japanese designer who founded Comme des Garçons in 1969, is only the second living designer honored by the Costume Institute, along with Saint Laurent.

“Rei Kawakubo is one of the most important and influential designers of the past 40 years,” said Costume Institute curator in charge Andrew Bolton in a press release. “By inviting us to rethink fashion as a site of constant creation, recreation, and hybridity, she has defined the aesthetics of our time.”

search_result.title: NYFW 2024 recap: All the fashion shows, events we ... - USA Today
search_result.text:
Feb. 18, 2024 Updated Feb. 22, 2024, 10:43 a.m. ET

NEW YORK — New York Fashion Week is officially over, and there was much to see and do.

Big designers like Christian Siriano and Sergio Hudson showed on the calendar, and celebrities from Beyoncé to Rachel Brosnahan and Danai Gurira made for buzzy front rows. With its new hub in Chelsea at the Starrett-Lehigh Building, fashion week still managed to take attendees across the city (and to Bushwick!) on a journey through snow and sartorial selections.

But this was not a season of reinvention or revolution. As designers and brands showed off their fall/winter 2024 collections, they leaned in to what makes clothing beautiful and stuck with doing that well. They didn't try to push bounds with virality or feign originality when everything's already been done. For fall, expect the return of the Big Belt, fur (both faux and real) in all lengths and colors, corsets and gloves of leather, tulle and fuzzy varieties.

Here's a recap of what (and whom) we saw at 22 runway shows, events, showrooms and installations.

L'Agence

While social media bemoans and critiques the influx of the "mob wife aesthetic," L'Agence inadvertently created a collection fit for the organized clothing of organized crime: cropped fur jackets, slinky, floor-length slips, deconstructed black tie and bejeweled pieces.

Need a news break? Check out the all new PLAY hub with puzzles, games and more!

Christian Siriano

Always one to kick off the calendar, Christian Siriano drafted lamé, bulbous sleeves and hemlines, and swaths of red for his journey through the desert of the future. Ashlee Simpson, "Succession" star J. Smith-Cameron, Melanie Lynskey, Busy Philipps, Sophia Bush and Siriano show staple Alicia Silverstone, sat front row.

Inside the fashion show at The Plaza: Christian Siriano taps Ashlee Simpson, this 'Succession' star for NYFW show

Phillip Lim and Hennessy Lunar New Year party

Jeannie Mai, Awkwafina, Kimora Lee Simmons' model daughter Aoki Lee Simmons and designer Phillip Lim himself (of 3.1 Phillip Lim) rang in the Lunar New Year in style at the Times Square Edition Hotel's Paradise Club, with takes on the highball and sidecar cocktails from Hennessy.

Custo Barcelona

If Zenon, of "Zenon: Girl of the 21st Century," and Michael Imperioli's "Sopranos" character had a child together who wanted to create clothing honoring their parents, this would be it. Custo Barcelona offered shimmering tracksuits, silver, purple and teal foil dresses and tinsel-topped separates (who exactly is wearing this remains to be seen).

Dennis Basso

The designer showcased his "return to going out and dressing up" at his New York store for the woman with uptown sensibilities — and pockets to afford the luxe pieces. I could absolutely see a particular slinky champagne lamé gown and corresponding cropped fur jacket on the Oscars afterparty circuit.

3.1 Phillip Lim

The designer's "Intersections" installation paired clothing and accessories hanging from the ceiling with multigenerational AAPI artists' work. Alongside celeb attendees Stephanie Hsu, Tyler Cameron, Jeannie Mai and Lauren Chan, I spotted a likely new It Bag, with a large flap and gold hardware.

Badgley Mischka

The Saturday morning show was a stunner, with standout moments including a swan-like gown and a chocolate brown sequin pantsuit. A little robot camera zooming to film the runway signaled a too-near future. And I got my first glimpse of "Gossip Girl" star Kelly Rutherford, who made the rounds at Tommy Hilfiger and Pamella Roland, too.

Black in Fashion Council Discovery Showroom

What's in a name. Providing "discovery" each season, this year's set of small, independent Black designers was a top-tier crop of talent.

Gvds, from Nigerian designer and former mechanical engineer Muktar Onifade, had the coolest and most cohesive creations I saw all week; Nia Thomas and Amari Carter, two self-titled brands, delivered high-quality sensuality to their knitwear; the colorful clothing and twin founders of BruceGlen brought their quintessential vibrance; and the Hermès Kelly bag may want to step aside for Silver and Riley's fabulous Italian leather New Yorker bags.

Lapointe

The girls were gagged when a big yellow school bus arrived outside the Lower East Side venue and models walked out to descend the runway. That's the fun of Lapointe, where bright colors and high-impact textural elements like sequins and fur mixed with collegiate touches.

Alice + Olivia

The clothes were cute — the line to get in was not. The brand has done an excellent job staying true to its roots and showcasing wearable, beautiful looks in a constantly evolving trend cycle and tough fashion market.

Nat Geo 'Fit for a Queen showcase

National Geographic threw its sartorial hat into the ring while promoting nature docuseries "Queens." The brief but impactful runway saw animal holograms paired with designs from JêBlanc, Sergio Hudson, Bohn Jsell, anOnlyChild, Head of State, Theophilio, Studio 189, Bed on Water, Almasika and L'Enchanteur (though the organization of both catwalk and crowd left something to be desired).

PatBO

A violinist and Black Swan ballerinas left attendees speechless. The pared-back collection explored the duality between light and dark, with a couple of deep teal gowns. An organizational nightmare, but at least wrapped in a dream and a good DJ at the afterparty. Also spotted: the "Pookie" couple who have taken TikTok for a ride.

Kallmeyer

Kallmeyer left me fed, double entendré intended. "So chic," said Sophia Bush as she walked in, succinctly summing up the minimalistic collection. AnnaSophia Robb and Whitney Port also showed up to support designer Daniella Kallmeyer.

Pamella Roland

Inspired by a trip to Belgium and its "enchanting hues" of wine, chocolate brown, bronze and gold, Pamella Roland stayed true to form with intricate gem, appliqué and feather work. And we were treated to a mini "America's Next Top Model" reunion with Eva Marcille, Nigel Barker and Jay Manuel.

Markarian

A short showroom visit was a moment of respite in a busy schedule, and a pale buttercream dress with gems down the center and across the waist added to the dreamy Markarian moment. The bridal offerings have not yet left my mind.

Retrofête

A fête indeed, if the party is an '80s business cocktail function where everyone is loaded and decadence reigns supreme. The Retrofête show was vibrant and featured statement colors we'll see more of come fall (bright red, cranberry, navy and pink-purple). It's what I imagine Margot Robbie's characters in "The Wolf of Wall Street" and "Barbie" might reach for on different days.

Sergio Hudson

Music: bumping. Hair: bouncing. Collection: bursting, with color, texture and coats galore. Sergio Hudson knows how to dress a boss woman, many of whom sat front row: Rachel Brosnahan, Janelle James, Karen Pittman, Hoda Kotb and Jenna Bush Hager, Danai Gurira and Sunny Hostin.

Bibhu Mohapatra

The designer reimagined "film noir, expressionist cinema, S&M and surrealism"; stylist and tastemaker June Ambrose and "Real Housewives of New York" newbie Jessel Taank sat front row.

LoveShackFancy

The frilly and feminine brand headed to a nightclub for its collection with a burlesque twist. Bubbly drinks and donuts inside corresponded with a much-need Dunkin' truck with coffee.

Luar

Beyoncé (and Solange and Ms. Tina Knowles) surprised everyone and the clothes were infinitely cool: What else is there to say? Raul Lopez's Luar is creating lore beyond signature bags: strong shoulders also draped with watches, wrists and waists adorned with jeweled cuffs and the introduction of a forthcoming line of basics.

Queen Bey is in the house: Beyoncé surprises with sparkling appearance at Luar show during NYFW

Brandon Maxwell

Happy Valentine's Day, indeed, from Brandon Maxwell, where bars of chocolate served as seat cards. The all-white set felt ethereal and otherworldly — then the first model emerged in a rippling cream gown that seemed to float down the runway, elevating the vibe to a more heavenly level. Pieces caressed the body, looking effortless in the way only a master designer could conjure.

Laraque

The new brand took visitors to the dollhouse, where a modern take on vintage looks awaited, as pinks and browns coincided with tulle and fur in delicate sets and dresses.

search_result.title: Met Gala's best dressed: Billie Eilish, Lil Nas X, Megan Fox, Rihanna
search_result.text:
Sept. 13, 2021 Updated Sept. 14, 2021, 6:13 a.m. ET

Fashion's biggest night is here and the biggest names in Hollywood didn't disappoint.

A-listers descended upon the Metropolitan Museum of Art's carpeted stairs in New York Monday, more than a year after the COVID-19 pandemic upended nearly all entertainment events and canceled the 2020 Met Gala.

The 2021 Met Gala is celebrating American fashion, just days after the 20th anniversary of 9/11 on Saturday, with the theme "In America: A Lexicon of Fashion." While many instantly think of stars and stripes or the colors red, white and blue, these stars took the theme to another level by paying tribute to icons (and even sports) quintessential to our country.

Here's a look at the 2021 Met Gala's best-dressed stars, including Jennifer Lopez and Lil Nas X, who embraced the American theme.

2021 Met Gala: From vaccines to co-chairs, here's everything we know

Need a news break? Check out the all new PLAY hub with puzzles, games and more!

Rihanna

Rihanna gave fashionably late a whole new meaning.

The red carpet ended without any sightings of the Queen of the Met Gala and with plenty of disappointment from fans. But as luck would have it, she miraculously appeared in an all-black look that resembled a large comforter. Remember Snuggies?

You may be wondering: How did she make the best-dressed list? Well, her look seemed appropriate following a year of hoodies and athleisure during the coronavirus pandemic.

Rihanna attended the Met Gala with A$AP Rocky, who appeared in a colorful blanket-like getup. This marks the couple's first red carpet appearance together.

Billie Eilish

Billie Eilish looked like the belle of the ball in a blush Oscar de la Renta gown that channeled American icons Marilyn Monroe, Grace Kelly and the Holiday Barbie Doll, which Eilish called her "favorite thing in the world."

Eilish's Met Gala look is a stark contrast to her longtime signature tomboy style, but she told Vogue host Keke Palmer that "it just was time for this" feminine look.

"I was scared. I didn’t feel comfortable in my skin and I finally do," said Eilish, who is serving as a co-chair alongside Timothée Chalamet, Amanda Gorman and Naomi Osaka.

2021 Met Gala co-chairs: Billie Eilish, Timothée Chalamet, Amanda Gorman and Naomi Osaka

Jennifer Lopez

JLo took the Met Gala to the Wild, Wild West in a Ralph Lauren gown, complete with a wide-brimmed hat, black feather jacket and a leather belt. The jet-setting actress, who just walked the red carpet at the Venice Film Festival days ago, was accompanied by Ben Affleck, although they didn't walk the red carpet together.

Jennifer Lopez and Ben Affleck share kiss, go red carpet official at Venice Film Festival

Lil Nas X

Move aside Lady Gaga. Lil Nas X debuted not one, not two, but three different outfits.

His first outfit featured a gold custom Atelier Versace robe fit for a king. He ditched the robe with the assistance of several helpers to reveal a suit of golden armor underneath. He followed it up with a skin-tight, embellished black and gold jumpsuit.

"We only get one chance to do this," he said. Might as well do it right.

Kendall Jenner

The supermodel paid homage to Audrey Hepburn in a custom sheer Givenchy gown dripping in jewels that is reminiscent of Hepburn's look in the 1964 film "My Fair Lady."

"My princess," sister Kylie Jenner wrote on her Instagram Story, after saying she was "so sad I couldn't make it this year." Kylie Jenner is expecting her second child with Travis Scott.

Megan Fox

The actress opted for a bright red Dundas cutout gown and sported sleek Bettie Page-inspired bangs. What makes it on-theme? Herself. "It’s on an American girl so it makes it American fashion," she said.

"I'm not afraid to be sexy," Fox told Palmer. "A woman who is intelligent and also knows how to weaponize her beauty… there's nothing more dangerous than that. There's nothing more powerful than that."

'I'm not afraid to be sexy': Megan Fox reveals thoughts on being Hollywood 'sex symbol' at Met Gala

Iman

Everyone, stop and bask in Iman's light. The supermodel looked like pure sunshine in her show-stopping Harris Reed gown. She said her Met Gala look "felt hopeful" and signifies a "ray of light after all the darkness we felt" in America the past year.

Reed accompanied Iman on the carpeted steps and said he chose Iman to wear his look because she's the "most iconic woman in the world."

Emily Blunt

The "Jungle Cruise" actress was dripping in Hollywood glamour while channeling actress Hedy Lamarr in a shimmery gown by Miu Miu that featured a sweeping white cape. She completed her look with a starry headdress similar to the one that Lamarr wore in the 1941 film "Ziegfeld Girl."

"I love all the opulence and boldness of old Hollywood," Blunt said.

Ciara

The singer took the Met Gala's nickname of "The Super Bowl of Fashion" to a new level, because what's more American than football?

Ciara payed homage to her NFL husband Russell Wilson, the Super Bowl-winning quarterback for the Seattle Seahawks, in a lime green, backless gown by Peter Dundas. Her look even featured Wilson's No. 3 jersey number.

And we can't forget about the accessories. In addition to her mini football clutch, Ciara said she's "also wearing his Super Bowl ring."

Carey Mulligan

Mulligan looked pretty in pink in a whimsical Valentino gown that her "Great Gatsby" character Daisy Buchanan would surely fawn over. Her ballgown featured a corseted top and massive train.

At least 104 dead as hard-hit areas in Texas face new flooding risks

Potent, slow-moving storms over central Texas have begun to trigger flooding in areas already hard-hit by the state. Forecasters with the National Weather Service extended some riverside flood watches and warnings, with some advisories lasting through Thursday.

Who's to blame for this tragedy?There is already a lot of finger pointing. The catastrophe was caused by a perfect storm of difficult-to-forecast rainfall and fast-moving water. Some wonder if budget cuts made things worse. Scientists say it was a terrible combination of terrain, timing and climate change.

Nearly a decade before this catastrophic flooding, the state’s Division of Emergency Management denied requests for a $1 million grant to improve Kerr County's flood warning system.

LA mayor calls out federal agent presence in MacArthur Park

'Franky, it is outrageous and un-American that we have federal armed vehicles in our parks when nothing is going on in our parks.'

~ Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass is condemning federal agents and National Guard troops for descending on a city park on Monday. Armored vehicles, mounted officers and armed troops briefly swarmed the city's MacArthur Park Monday morning before leaving. Bass said 20 children were playing before the troops surged through and shared footage of federal officers running through the park.

Are tariff rates going up?

President Donald Trump in April announced a baseline 10% tariff, as well as targeted tariffs, on dozens of countries. After stocks tanked and the bond market rebelled, Trump reversed course and said he would pause the sweeping tariffs for 90 days, giving countries until July 9 to negotiate rates. The flat 10% rate remained in effect. But the July 9 goalpost has been pushed back — with new tariff rates set to kick in on Aug. 1. So far, the Trump administration has announced two trade deals, with Vietnam and the United Kingdom. Because U.S. businesses that import will be paying Trump's tariffs, economists have warned that U.S. consumers will likely see higher prices as companies pass along those higher rates to customers.

search_result.title: Michaels acquires Joann brands: What customers can expect
search_result.text:
June 5, 2025 Updated June 6, 2025, 9:59 a.m. ET

Michaels is hoping to sew up the business of fans who frequented the fabric and craft chain Joann, which has folded all of its stores after filing for bankruptcy.

Michaels has acquired Joann's intellectual property and private label brands – including the Big Twist brands – the craft retailer announced Thursday, June 5.

Michaels will expand its fabric, sewing, and yarn assortment by adding more than 600 products including Brother and Singer sewing machines, Oliso Smart Irons and needle crafting kits from DMC and Loops & Threads.

"This acquisition allows us to better serve both new and existing customers, respond to rising demand across categories, and build on our momentum as the destination for creating and celebrating in North America," said Michaels CEO David Boone in a press release.

Michaels unfolds bigger move into fabrics: What customers can expect

The Joann's acquisition meshes with Michaels' evolving consumer interests. Over the past year, more customers have been searching on Michaels.com for "fabric" and "sewing," according to the company's announcement.

Recently, Michaels has increased the fabric assortment in more than 680 of its stores and plans to do so in 280 additional stores this year, the company said. This month, the retailer, which has more than 1,300 stores in 49 states and Canada, is also expanding its sewing supplies.

Spend your money smart: Sign up for USA TODAY's Daily Money newsletter.

Also growing: Michaels' yarn assortment – set to increase by 25% this year to meet shoppers' demands. Starting this month, Michaels will begin offering 60 new Loops & Threads yarns in stores and online. This summer, yarn collections from Bernat, Lion Brand and Red Heart will be available.

Products from Joann brands such as Big Twist Value Plus, Big Twist Twinkle, Big Twist Posh and Big Twist Baby Bear will be available in-stores and online later this year, according to the company.

Joann, which had operated about 800 stores, began closing them after filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in January 2025, its second bankruptcy filing in less than a year.

Michaels, which began wooing Joann customers in early May, has also created a dedicated landing page for those shoppers to make it easier for them to find products and know the latest updates.

"We're honored to have the opportunity to welcome Joann customers into our creative community and are committed to delivering the selection, value, and inspiration they are looking for at Michaels," Boone said in a statement.

Mike Snider is a reporter on USA TODAY’s Trending team. You can follow him on Threads, Bluesky, X and email him at mikegsnider & @mikegsnider.bsky.social & @mikesnider & msnider@usatoday.com

search_result.title: Typhoon threatens Taiwan as record storm drought ends - USA Today
search_result.text:
Doyle Rice

USA TODAY

Updated July 5, 2016, 1:01 p.m. ET

Taiwan is bracing for the arrival of Typhoon Nepartak, which could hit the island nation later in the week with wind gusts up to 172 mph, according to the Joint Typhoon Warning Center.

Typhoons are the same types of storms as hurricanes. They're called typhoons west of the International Date Line.

Currently at Category 4 strength, with 140 mph winds, the storm is forecast to become a Category 5 storm within the next 24 hours, WeatherBell meteorologist Ryan Maue said. It could impact northern Taiwan late Thursday, he said.

In Taiwan, the Taipei city government said it started typhoon safety measures such as clearing drainage systems and securing trees, China Postreported.

Ferry companies suspended passenger services to and from Orchid Island, located off the east coast of Taiwan, through Saturday, Taipei Times reported.

As of Tuesday afternoon, Nepartak was centered about 1,000 miles southeast of Taiwan's southernmost tip, moving west-northwest at a speed of 20 mph, Taiwan's Central Weather Bureau reported.

It's the first typhoon of the year. When Nepartak formed Sunday, it ended an all-time record storm drought in the northwest Pacific Ocean. It had been 200 days since a tropical storm or typhoon had formed in that part of the world, according to Colorado State University hurricane expert Phil Klotzbach.

That broke the previous storm-free stretch of 198 days, he said. The North Pacific is the most active ocean for tropical cyclones — hurricanes and typhoons — in the world.

Nepartak is the name of a Micronesian warrior.

A bit closer to home, Hurricane Blas is spinning harmlessly in the eastern Pacific Ocean, the National Hurricane Center said. Although it's expected to strengthen to a Category 3 hurricane, it poses no threat to land and will dissipate far out at sea by the end of the week.

There are no tropical storms or hurricanes anywhere in the Atlantic, and none are expected to form for at least the next two days, the hurricane center predicted.

search_result.title: House Republicans brace for doomsday scenario if McCarthy falls ...
search_result.text:
As a right-wing faction threatens to tank his speakership ambitions, House GOP leader Kevin McCarthy delivered a promise: “I’ll never leave,” making clear he has no plans to drop out of the race even if the fight goes to many ballots on the floor.

“I’ll get 218,” McCarthy told CNN, referring to the votes he’d need to become House speaker.

But Rep. Andy Biggs of Arizona, a conservative hardliner who is challenging McCarthy to be the most powerful member of Congress, doubled down on his commitment to stop the California Republican’s ascension.

“I’m not bluffing,” Biggs told CNN on Thursday when asked if he would drop out.

With the increasing likelihood that the speaker’s race could go to multiple ballots – something that hasn’t happened since 1923 – McCarthy’s allies and foes alike are starting to quietly game out the next steps if he can’t get the necessary 218 votes on the first round and they move into uncharted territory.

Video Ad Feedback Bash asks Pelosi if McCarthy has what it takes to be House Speaker. See her response 2:37 • Source: CNN Bash asks Pelosi if McCarthy has what it takes to be House Speaker. See her response 2:37

McCarthy’s supporters are vowing to keep voting for him on multiple ballots, and GOP sources said there are early discussions about a floor strategy for that potential scenario, including whether to recess the House or let the votes keep rolling – no matter how long it takes.

To prevent that from happening, McCarthy and his team have been engaged in serious talks with a group of conservatives, including over potentially giving them influential committee assignments and more power to drive the legislative process. GOP sources said those negotiations are still early in the process and could ultimately end up giving the group some aspect of what the hardliners desperately want: additional power to seek a sitting speaker’s ouster with a vote on the floor.

Asked if he would drop out of the race if he doesn’t get 218 votes on the first ballot, Biggs refused to say.

“I’m not going to talk about hypotheticals,” said Biggs, who lost his conference’s nomination to become speaker last month after securing 31 votes.

But in the case of a doomsday scenario – where neither McCarthy nor Biggs can get 218 votes on January 3 and neither drops out – some pro-McCarthy Republicans are signaling support for a different approach. Some said they would be willing to work with Democrats to find a moderate Republican who can get the 218 votes to clinch the gavel – a long-shot idea that underscores the uncertainty looming over the speaker’s race.

“Our initial plan is vote for Kevin and let him fight this out repeatedly. … But if they think they’re going to use this to infinity to drive him out, well, we’re not going to bend to their will,” said Rep. Don Bacon, a Nebraska Republican.

Bacon added if GOP hardliners don’t bend, then he would be willing to work with Democrats to find another more moderate Republican to secure the 218 votes to become speaker.

“If a small group refuses to play ball and be part of the team, then we’ll work across the aisle to find an agreeable Republican,” Bacon said. “But I hope we don’t get there.”

McCarthy’s detractors don’t buy it.

“There are very significant rules, changes being discussed that would open the House up, that would be transformative, that would give us the ability to actually legislate and represent our constituents,” said Rep. Matt Gaetz, a Republican from Florida who said he’s a “hard no” on McCarthy. “And whoever is speaker is going to have to agree to those rules, I think. And I don’t think that person will be Kevin McCarthy because Kevin McCarthy won’t have 218 votes.”

Gaetz added: “I think the person who is ultimately going to be the speaker isn’t even the candidate yet.”

Indeed, the small group of Republicans known as the “Never Kevin” movement – confident that Biggs could not win a majority of the House – has been trying to recruit a viable alternative, and claim “several” Republicans have privately told them they would be interested in running if McCarthy drops out. Their goal with voting for Biggs is to show that McCarthy is weak on the first ballot, which they hope would inspire other candidates to jump in.

“How many members vote for someone else will show the strength (of the anti-McCarthy group),” Rep. Bob Good, a Virginia Republican who is a “hard no” on McCarthy, told CNN. “I think the second ballot is going to have more candidates. … There are already Republicans letting us know they’d like to be considered.”

Even House Republicans who are supporting McCarthy predicted that a number of lawmakers would run if McCarthy withdrew his name, with some saying that House Minority Whip Steve Scalise, McCarthy’s top deputy, would emerge as the front runner in that case.
