He described donning the proper safety equipment, taking an elevator ride down to the mine, and then a mantrip ride by rail to the longwall to watch a machine grind and extract coal from the seam. Walsh’s agency oversees the Mine Safety and Health Administration as well as the administration of benefits for coal miners disabled by black lung disease. “I felt it was really important for me to go down and get a feel for what mine workers do,” Walsh said. “I have a different understanding and appreciation of the work.” Democratic candidates for president have struggled in recent years to connect with voters in West Virginia, in part due to a push toward clean energy under the Obama administration. Still, despite his promises, coal did not come roaring back under Republican President Donald Trump.

Wisconsin

Wisconsin Dells: The Mount Olympus Water and Theme Park is building the nation’s first rotating waterslide as part of a $23 million expansion. The project includes a new 22,500-square-foot building attached to the indoor waterpark. The rotating waterslide, named Medusa’s Slidewheel, is a four-person raft ride that combines the rotation of a Ferris wheel with the propulsion of a waterslide, according to a news release from architecture and engineer firm Ramaker and Associates, which is working on the project. Outside the United States, the only other rotating waterslides are in China and Poland, the news release said. Mount Olympus announced the project on its Facebook page Aug. 6. Construction began in July and is scheduled to be completed by Memorial Day 2022. “It will be the coolest slide that ever hit the waterpark industry,” Mount Olympus CEO and owner Nick Laskaris told WiscNews. “Not just the Dells, but the industry as a whole.” In addition to Medusa’s Slidewheel, the indoor waterpark expansion project will include a large swimming pool area, Laskaris told WiscNews. The existing indoor waterpark will be remodeled to match the new addition, he said.

Wyoming

Cheyenne: Marijuana advocates plan to begin circulating petitions in September for two proposed ballot measures – one asking if the state should legalize medical marijuana, and the other if it should decriminalize pot. The Wyoming Attorney General’s Office this week approved the wording of the proposed questions. The Secretary of State’s Office has now begun the process of seeking bids for printing the petitions. The process ends Aug. 25. The Wyoming chapter of the National Organization for the Reform of Marijuana Laws plans to begin gathering signatures on or soon after Sept. 1. “We’ll be hitting events, going door to door. We intend to get it all wrapped up by February,” Wyoming NORML Executive Director Bennett Sondeno said Wednesday. The group has a “big list” of Wyoming events where it plans to gather signatures in the next few months, Sondeno said. February is the deadline to submit enough valid signatures to the Wyoming secretary of state to get the measures before voters in the 2022 general election, Sondeno said. Wyoming is among a minority of states that don’t allow cannabis in some fashion.

From USA TODAY Network and wire reports

search_result.title: Chip shortage, explained: Why it's impacting car prices, tech items
search_result.text:
Michael Liedtke, Tom Krisher

Associated Press

Updated April 2, 2021, 3:05 p.m. ET

SAN RAMON, Calif. – As the U.S. economy rebounds from its pandemic slump, a vital cog is in short supply: the computer chips that power a wide range of products that connect, transport and entertain us in a world increasingly dependent on technology.

The shortage has already been rippling through various markets since last summer. It has made it difficult for schools to buy enough laptops for students forced to learn from home, delayed the release of popular products such as the iPhone 12 and created mad scrambles to find the latest video game consoles such as the PlayStation 5.

But things have been getting even worse in recent weeks, particularly in the auto industry, where factories are shutting down because there aren’t enough chips to finish building vehicles that are starting to look like computers on wheels. The problem was recently compounded by a grounded container ship that blocked the Suez Canal for nearly a week, choking off chips headed from Asia to Europe.

Computer chip shortages proliferate: Why you could soon pay more for a used car

More checks going out: Social Security recipients now receiving stimulus checks

These snags are likely to frustrate consumers who can’t find the vehicle they want and sometimes find themselves settling for a lower-end models without as many fancy electronic features. And it threatens to leave a big dent in the auto industry, which by some estimates stands to lose $60 billion in sales during the first half of his year.

“We have been hit by the perfect storm, and it’s not going away any time soon,” said Baird technology analyst Ted Mortonson, who said he has never seen such a serious shortage in nearly 30 years tracking the chip industry.

Is the pandemic to blame?

Sort of. The pandemic prompted chip factories to start shutting down early last year, particularly overseas, where the majority of the processors are made. By the time they started to reopen, they had a backlog of orders to fill.

That wouldn’t have been as daunting if chipmakers weren’t then swamped by unforeseen demand. For instance, no one entered 2020 expecting to see a spike in personal computer sales after nearly a decade of steady decline. But that’s what happened after government lockdowns forced millions of office workers to do their jobs from home while students mostly attended their classes remotely.

Are there other factors at work?

Yes. Both Sony and Microsoft were preparing to release highly anticipated next-generation video game consoles for their PlayStation and Xbox brands, respectively, that required more sophisticated chips than ever. To add to the demand, wireless network providers are clamoring for chips to power ultrafast “5G” services being built around the world.

Former President Donald Trump’s trade war with China probably didn’t help either. Some analysts believe the Trump administration’s blacklisting of Huawei Technologies prompted that major maker of smartphones to build a huge stockpile of chips as it braced for the crackdown.

Why is the auto industry being hit so hard?

Stay-at-home orders drove a surge in consumer electronics sales, squeezing auto parts suppliers who use chips for computers that control gas pedals, transmissions and touch screens. Chipmakers compounded the pressure by rejiggering factory lines to better serve the consumer electronics market, which generates far more revenue for them than autos.

After eight weeks of pandemic-induced shutdown in the spring, automakers started reopening factories earlier than they had envisioned. But then they were hit with unexpected news: chipmakers weren’t able to flip a switch quickly and make the types of processors needed for cars.

How are automakers dealing with the shortage?

They’ve canceled shifts and temporarily closed factories. Ford, General Motors, Fiat Chrysler (now Stellantis), Volkswagen and Honda seem to have been hit the hardest. Others, most notably Toyota, aren’t being affected as dramatically. That is probably because Toyota was better prepared after learning how sudden, unexpected shocks can disrupt supply chains from the massive earthquake and tsunami that hit Japan in 2011, said Bank of America Securities analyst Vivek Arya.

The harder-hit automakers have diverted chips from slower-selling models to those in high demand, such as pickup trucks and large SUVs. Ford, GM and Stellantis have started building vehicles without some computers, putting them in storage with plans to retrofit them later.

GM expects the chip shortage to cost it up to $2 billion in pretax profits this year from lost production and sales. Ford is bracing for a similar blow. Chipmakers probably won’t fully catch up with auto-industry demand until July at the earliest.

Will this impact my ability to buy a new car this year?

Expect to pay more. Supplies of many models were tight even before the chip shortage because automakers were having trouble making up for production lost to the pandemic.

IHS Markit estimates that from January through March, the chip shortage reduced North American auto production by about 100,000 vehicles. In January of last year, before the pandemic, the U.S. auto industry had enough vehicles to supply 77 days of demand. By this February, it was down almost 30% to 55 days.

Will other popular products be affected this year?

Samsung Electronics, one of the world’s biggest chipmakers, recently warned that its vast lineup of consumer electronics could be affected by the shortage. Without specifying which products might be affected, Samsung co-CEO Koh Dong-jin told shareholders that a “serious imbalance” between the supply and demand for chips could hurt sales from April through June.

What will prevent another chip shortage?

There are no quick fixes, but chipmakers appear to be gearing up to meet future challenges.

Intel, which for decades has dominated the market for PC chips, recently made waves by announcing plans to invest $20 billion in two new factories in Arizona. Even more significant, Intel revealed said it is starting a new division that will enter into contracts to make chips tailored for other firms in addition to its own processors. That’s a major departure for Intel, aligning it more closely with a model popularized by Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., or TSMC, which already had been building a plant in Arizona, too.

Compelled by the current shortage, TSMC also has committed to spending $100 billion during the next three years to expand its worldwide chip manufacturing capacity. About $28 billion of that investment will come this year to boost production at factories that have been unable to keep up with the surge in demand since the pandemic began, according to TSMC Chief Executive Officer C.C. Wei.

And President Joe Biden’s $2 trillion plan to improve U.S. infrastructure includes an estimated $50 billion to help make the country less reliant on chips made overseas. The U.S. share of the worldwide chip manufacturing market has declined from 37% in 1990 to 12% today, according to Semiconductor Industry Association, a trade group.

But chips won’t start coming out of any new factories built as part of the spending splurge for two to three years. And even as existing factories ramp up and expand to meet current demand, some analysts wonder if there might be a glut of processors a year from now.

Krisher reported from Detroit.

search_result.title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce are married: Wedding recap - USA Today
search_result.text:
9:50 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update 'Elizabeth Taylor, do you think it's forever?' Bryan West It seems Portofino is always on the singer-songwriter's mind. The Elizabeth Taylor AIDS Foundation shared a congratulatory message on Instagram: "Before MSG hosted this amazing wedding, it hosted Elizabeth Taylor." The post recounted an event on Oct. 17, 1957, when producer Mike Todd threw his wife a lavish celebration for 18,000 guests inside Madison Square Garden, complete with a 14-foot cake, Duke Ellington and Walter Cronkite broadcasting live on CBS. Swift recently paid tribute to the Hollywood icon with an entire song on The Life of a Showgirl and licensed imagery and other intellectual property from Elizabeth Taylor's estate for the music video.In a New York Times interview, Swift explained her deep affection for the late actress: "I love Elizabeth Taylor so much. She fought for artists’ rights. She was exploited in so many ways, and yet she kept her humanity. She kept her humor. She kept her passion for life."

9:37 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update It's been 1,013 days Bryan West Is the clowning ever really over? Is it intentionally masterminded or a cosmic coincidence? July 3, 2026 – the day of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding celebration – comes exactly 1,013 days after Swift made her headline-making first appearance at a Chiefs game on Sept. 24, 2023, when she sat beside Donna Kelce at Arrowhead Stadium as Kansas City defeated the Chicago Bears. 1,013 not only contains her favorite number, 13, but it mirrors the runtime of Swift's longest song, the 10-minute version of "All Too Well" (10:13).

9:14 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update New York got Taylor 'something blue' Liz Schubauer The Empire State Building joined in on the Swift-Kelce wedding festivities by announcing it illuminated its tower in light blue in order to serve as the bride's "something blue." It's the second love story the New York City landmark has participated in this week. On July 1, Russian daredevils climbed to the tip of the building's spire before getting engaged, and then arrested.

8:11 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update It's a rain show Bryan West Moments after Swift and Kelce exchanged vows, rain and flashes of lightning swept across Manhattan outside Madison Square Garden. Swift fans will tell you, especially the ones who've been stuck in open roofed stadiums, that Eras Tour "rain shows" were some of the best. During the final outdoor rain show of the tour in Miami, she called them "very, very special." Rain has a similar reputation in wedding folklore. It symbolizes good fortune, cleansing and a marriage built to last. One popular saying suggests a knot tied on a rainy day is harder to untie. When the rain cleared, a rainbow arched above the New York skyline.

7:52 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Adam Sandler officiates Taylor Swift, Travis wedding Melissa Ruggieri He wasn't there as "The Wedding Singer," but Adam Sandler played a pivotal role in the Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce marriage. Swift's representative, Tree Paine, confirmed that the actor-comedian officiated the ceremony at Madison Square Garden. Swift and Sandler have been friends for the past few years, and the singer has enjoyed a friendship with Sunny and Sadie, Sandler's daughters with wife Jackie. Kelce, meanwhile, is close enough to Sandler that he made a shirtless cameo in last year's "Happy Gilmore 2" as a restaurant manager who fires a busboy (played by Bad Bunny). FULL STORY: Why Adam Sandler officiated the Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce wedding

7:50 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update MSG displays 'JUST&T MARRIED!' About 7:30 p.m. ET, the digital signs outside Madison Square Garden lit up with an announcement: "JUST&T MARRIED!" Word soon spread it was official: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are now husband and wife.

7:45 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Who was Travis Kelce's best man? Wedding party revealed Liz Schubauer A spokesperson for Swift confirmed that the couple did not have bridesmaids or groomsmen. Instead, their brothers were part of the ceremony. Austin Swift served as Taylor's man of honor, while Jason Kelce was Travis' best man.

7:31 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are married! Bryan West Your English teacher and your gym teacher are officially married. 🧨 Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have said "I do" at Madison Square Garden in front of a sea of celebrities, athletes, business moguls, friends and family. FULL STORY: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce marry in Madison Square Garden wedding

6:43 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Silver Chevy SUV believed to be carrying Taylor Swift arrives at MSG Around 6:30 p.m., a silver Chevrolet Suburban SUV departed Taylor Swift's residence and arrived at Madison Square Garden. It's believed the pop star may be inside the vehicle.

6:33 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update No sleep? No problem for the big party Ralphie Aversa There are reports that the Taylor-Travis event at MSG could last until 2 a.m. But like any good party, there might also be an after-party. At least one guest was overheard at a midtown Manhattan hotel explaining his situation: He's anticipating things might not wrap up until much later in the evening, and he has an early flight out of New York City. His solution? Keep the party going until its time to leave for the airport. It could amount to almost a 12-hour fiesta when it's all said and done, which would certainly line up with the moniker "Wedding of the Century." Arrivals were witnessed as early as 2:50 p.m. at the Garden, with a slew of guests leaving their hotels between 3-3:30 p.m.

6:31 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Speak now or forever hold your peace? Ralphie Aversa It's 100 degrees in New York City on the July 4 weekend and 7th Avenue is shut down in front of Penn Station. There was at least one person who thought to protest over the situation. An unidentified woman wearing sunglasses and a face covering made her way down 7th Avenue behind the media pen across from Madison Square Garden. The woman shouted while she held a sign that called Swift "truly a monster" for holding an event at a large venue in Manhattan during the historic heatwave. New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced that cooling centers would be open across the city due to the high temperatures. Earlier in the week, the mayor asked New Yorkers to set their air conditioning to 78 degrees as to not overwhelm the power grid. While in line with recent guidance from the Department of Energy, the recommendation drew the ire of a number of Republicans. Contributing: Amethyst Martinez, USA TODAY NETWORK

6:27 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Karlie Kloss surprises at Taylor Swift wedding Charlie Carballo Karlie Kloss made a gilded arrival. The supermodel, who is a longtime friend to Swift, wore a strapless, liquid-gold satin column gown with a softly draped neckline, accessorizing with a metallic clutch. Her husband, tech entrepreneur Joshua Kushner, complemented the look in a double-breasted tuxedo. Kloss, who recently attended one of Swift's Eras Tour stops in Los Angeles, was also featured in the singer's 2015 "Bad Blood" music video. FULL STORY: Karlie Kloss and Joshua Kushner attend Taylor Swift wedding after fallout rumors

6:03 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Country stars spotted on way to wedding Melissa Ruggieri and Liz Schubauer A gaggle of country music stars was spotted in New York ahead of the wedding. Maren Morris, Kelsea Ballerini, and members of Little Big Town and Sugarland were photographed together. Miranda Lambert and her husband were seen as well.

5:57 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Ed Sheeran shocks in bright blue for Taylor Swift wedding Charlie Carballo Ed Sheeran, who's been rumored to perform at the wedding festivities, made his way to MSG wearing a bright blue suit alongside wife Cherry Seaborn. Full story: Ed Sheeran arrives at Taylor Swift's wedding in bright suit

5:52 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Zoe Kravitz shimmers on the way to Taylor Swift wedding Charlie Carballo Zoë Kravitz wore a sheer, nude-toned cocktail dress adorned with intricate gold and silver beaded floral embroidery, pairing the sparkling look with black accessories and strappy heels.

5:51 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Camila Cabello, Sombr, Benson Boone, more music stars arrive at wedding Charlie Carballo Camila Cabello was pretty in pink, showing a brief glimpse of her gown while making her way to MSG in the back of a car. Benson Boone donned a bowtie with his tux on the way into the Garden. Singer-songwriter Sombr shared a TikTok video where he's seen suiting up and "feeling gorgeous rn" ahead of the wedding. The Chainsmokers duo Alex Pall and Drew Taggart posed for a photo while suited up ahead of the wedding. British singer Ellie Goulding was spotted entering a car while wearing a black cocktail dress.

5:32 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Jason Sudeikis arrives at MSG Liz Schubauer and Charlie Carballo Jason Sudeikis, wearing a tuxedo, made his arrival to the wedding. Kansas City is his hometown, and he's a longtime Chiefs fan.

3:47 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Taylor Swift wedding guests begin arriving at Madison Square Garden Leigh Cuen and Bryan West At least three people are checking iPads for guests lists in front of the arena-turned-wedding venue as more than 50 cars and buses wait to offload guests at Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding celebration. People are showing their phones to security before entering. Actress Dakota Johnson was spotted in a black gown, while Travis' longtime friend Joe Orave posted a photo of his tux via Instagram. Meanwhile, Swift's childhood friend Abigail Anderson was spotted entering a black SUV wearing a dress with a floor-length chocolate brown skirt. “Modern Family” actor Eric Stonestreet was seen making his way to a car with wife Lindsay Schweitzer, who was wearing a shimmery dusty rose metallic evening dress. Tommy Hilfiger and his wife, Dee Ocleppo Hilfiger, posed for a photo in the back of their car, with Ocleppo Hilfiger wearing a vibrant red gown accented by an oversized turquoise shoulder bow and matching floral earrings. Kansas City Chiefs running back Kareem Hunt was seen making his way to the event. "Shark Tank" investor Barbara Corcoran, who has been a longtime Taylor Swift fan, teased that she’s “getting ready for a very special wedding… such a love story.” Roughly 1,000 wedding guests are expected to continue arriving until 5 p.m. ET and may stay to party until the early hours of the morning on Independence Day. Supermodel Gigi Hadid, wearing pink, and among the guests continuing to trickle in, as well as bestselling author Jenny Han and actor Bradley Cooper. Read more

3:25 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Taylor Swift wedding livestream: See outside Madison Square Garden

2:17 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update From cakes to candy, brands embrace the Swift-Kelce wedding hype Caché McClay Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are expected to tie the knot today. So business owners, fans and everyone else in between are trying to get in on the action. From candy makers to bakers and wedding favor brands, people and companies around the world appear to be capitalizing on what could be the biggest wedding of the era. While not yet verified, cake designer Julie Deffense shared what appeared to be the couple's wedding cake on Instagram, saying that it traveled more than 1,200 miles for the highly anticipated event. Deffense operates out of both Portugal and Florida. The caption read: "A very special cake delivery for a very special couple! Can’t wait to share more soon!" Along those same lines, a company named Sticky Lollies claimed to create custom candies for the soon-to-be married couple, sharing photos of the treats on Instagram. "Wedding custom orders are the best! Being apart of peoples special day is so lovely for us. Thank you to Taylor and Travis for their custom order, hope you have a great love story," the company wrote. Meanwhile, in Kansas City, the Instagram account for Missouri's historic landmark that was once a train station also posted about the famous couple. "Tonight, we light up Union Station in orange and teal for Taylor Swift and red and gold for Travis Kelce," the Instagram account, which might typically focus on promoting the station's planetarium and science center, posted on the night of July 2. Businesses and craftsmen around the world are continuing to post congratulations for the soon-to-be newlyweds.

2:07 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Jason and Travis Kelce post special 'New Heights' episode with Prince William Jack McKessy The Kelce brothers released a special episode of their "New Heights" podcast on July 3 featuring Prince William, heir apparent to the British throne, as their guest. While the reported wedding between Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift was never mentioned, Prince William did joke about the Kansas City Chiefs tight end's "most iconic moment at Wembley Stadium" in London. Jason asked the prince which of Travis' most notable performances at Wembley was more iconic: a touchdown he scored against the Lions at the stadium in 2015, or his appearance as a backup dancer for Swift during her Eras Tour's Wembley stop. "Travis as a backup dancer," Prince William answered. "I think you should do it again." During the rest of the episode, most of the talk focused on Prince William's soccer fandom and the English national team's performance at the 2026 World Cup. The Prince of Wales said he's "quietly confident" in this year's squad after their round of 32 win over DR Congo on July 1. He also said he hopes to see the Kelce brothers at the World Cup final if England makes it to the match, which will take place at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey, on July 19.

1:15 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Wedding guests leave with monogrammed gift boxes Leigh Cuen Although both Swift and Kelce have yet to confirm the reports, People and Page Six have reported that guests attended the couple's rehearsal diner on July 2 in New York City. Some of those guests, such as football player Ross Travis, were seen leaving the Madison Square Garden area with monogrammed velvet boxes. The image was shared by the celebrity news aggregation Instagram account @deuxmoi, which credited the pop culture outlet @backgrid_usa with finding the photo. It remains to be seen what the superstar couple is giving their guests as wedding favors.

1:00 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Wedding schedule reports emerge Bryan West The New York Times, CNN and ABC reported the couple are expected to host a rehearsal event July 2, followed by a larger celebration on July 3 that could last until 4 a.m. and host roughly 1,000 guests. Neither Swift nor Kelce has publicly confirmed the plans.

12:53 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Trump's White House shares Taylor Swift-themed post ahead of wedding Jennifer Hassan Amid Swift-Kelce wedding fever, Trump found a (blank?) space to insert himself into the holiday weekend mania, taking to social media and sharing an image of himself at the center of an Eras Tour-themed political poster. "It's been a long time coming…," the official White House account wrote in a post on X Thursday, July 2, the same day stars gathered in Maddison Square Garden for what was reportedly the pre-wedding rehearsal dinner. The White House's choice of caption is a lyric from Swift's track "Miss Americana & the Heartbreak Prince." In the X post, Trump features in the middle of the poster, surrounded by other historic figures including Abraham Lincoln and George Washington. "America's Eras Tour" reads the text underneath the image, in reference to Swift's record-breaking Eras Tour. Full story: Trump jumps on Taylor Swift wedding fever with 'America's Eras Tour'

12:36 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Who is designing Taylor Swift's wedding dress? Morgan Evans While Swift has not revealed who designed her gown – and there may be several – there are several possibilities based on designers she's frequently worn throughout her career. Among the names drawing the most attention: Monse, the New York brand founded by Laura Kim and Fernando Garcia (Swift wore some of their looks when they served as co-creative directors at Oscar de la Renta); Stella McCartney (she's rumored to be attending the wedding); Jonathan Anderson of Christian Dior; Vivienne Westwood, known for romantic corseted bridal gowns; Ralph Lauren, whose striped dress Swift wore in her engagement announcement; Oscar de la Renta, a longtime red-carpet favorite; Jackson Wiederhoeft, whose designs Swift has recently embraced; and Givenchy under creative director Sarah Burton. All our predictions: Who's designing Taylor Swift's wedding dress? Here are 5 possibilities

12:19 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Donna Kelce arrives in New York City: Report Anthony Robledo Donna Kelce, the mother of the NFL star, has arrived in New York City ahead of the rumored ceremony, People reported. Swift's soon-to-be mother-in-law, 73, arrived in the city on Thursday, July 2, amid reports that a wedding rehearsal dinner would occur later that night, according to People. The "Traitors" star is also the mother of former Philadelphia Eagles center and "New Heights" podcaster Jason Kelce, 38. On June 24, Jason told reporters he was "Obviously very excited, very excited for my brother. Obviously, a big moment for him. " "I'm very happy for both of them and looking forward to celebrating with them. It's a good year right now, and it's a good year for both of them as well," he said.

12:09 pm ET July 3, 2026 Share this update A red, white and Taylor Swift weekend Melissa Ruggieri Hardly anyone is talking about the Great American State Fair, unless it's about the light crowds, short lines and acres of open space. But nearly everyone is talking about Taylor Swift and the "wedding of the century." The Fourth of July weekend is the platform for patriotic pride, and this year, Miss Americana will get the headlines, the photo spreads, the last word in her skillfully planned celebration of love. She'll also get to celebrate one of her favorite holidays, as documented through the years from her Rhode Island mansion, in the grandest way possible. Swift is no fan of President Donald Trump, which she confirmed by her endorsement of Kamala Harris during the 2024 election, writing in a post that she was voting for a leader who projects "calm and not chaos." No one except for Swift and Kelce and their inner circle knows if the – again, purported – nuptials were inked for the same weekend as the 250th anniversary of America as a deliberate jab at the administration or if the dates just happened to coincide. But whether this Swift-apalooza was slated to intentionally grab headlines away from Trump blustering through a weekend of red, white and blue-themed activities or is merely an amusingly timed coincidence, there is no doubt the Commander in Chief will spew a late-night word jumble toward the woman who dared steal his spotlight. Swift will either ignore the noise as usual, or smile at the absurdity as a secure, proud childless cat lady. Read more

11:47 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Why is Taylor Swift getting married in New York? Bryan West Few venues in North America offer what Madison Square Garden already has built in: security. Andrea Davis, president and CEO of The Resiliency Initiative, has spent 25 years planning emergency response and crisis management for organizations including Disney, Walmart, the FIFA World Cup and Times Square's New Year's Eve celebration. "Madison Square Garden is a venue known for their security protocol," Davis says. "It's top-notch. When I heard about it being an inside venue – and especially being at Madison – I thought, 'Oh, that just takes all of those security issues off the table.'" The arena also already has experienced personnel, and established procedures for protecting high-profile guests and trusted vendors and staff are accustomed to strict nondisclosure agreements.

11:24 am ET July 3, 2026 Share this update Madison Square Garden and Taylor Swift have history Bryan West Madison Square Garden does hold personal significance for Swift. search_result.title: Why Madison Square Garden makes sense for Taylor Swift wedding
search_result.text:
July 1, 2026 Updated July 2, 2026, 3:04 p.m. ET

In "The Lucky One," Taylor Swift sings about a character choosing "the rose garden over Madison Square," imagining a life of privacy winning over fame.

Fourteen years later, the lyric has taken on an unexpected new meaning: a rose garden proposal and, if reports are accurate, a Madison Square Garden wedding celebration.

Although neither Swift or Travis Kelce, nor their representatives, have publicly commented, the possibility of a wedding at one of the world's most iconic sports and entertainment venues has become the subject of intense speculation. Poetically, Madison Square Garden embodies the marriage of their two worlds: the home of championship sports and legendary entertainment.

Since initial reports, internet sleuths have pieced together Broadway schedules of potential guests, Chiefs training camp dates, radio interviews and venue calendars trying to determine whether the reports are accurate.

NYC gets heated: All the Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding prep details

Need a news break? Check out the all new PLAY hub with puzzles, games and more!

It may still be possible that the famous couple plans to tie the knot in a private, family affair, then celebrate afterwards with a glitzy party in Manhattan. Fellow billionaires Beyoncé Knowles-Carter and JAY-Z Carter, for example, got married in their Tribeca home. Swift also owns a large compound in Tribeca, though it would not be able to accommodate the droves of people expected to attend.

More recently, actors Zendaya and Tom Holland had a secret wedding ceremony they only announced later. The rose garden proposal happened in Kelce's private residence, which may offer a precedent for the couple celebrating their nuptials at home too.

Whether the Madison Square Gardens rumor are true are or not, one thing has become clear: the iconic New York venue makes more sense from a security standpoint than many people first realized.

Madison Square Garden offers optimal security

If security is the priority, few venues in North America offer what Madison Square Garden already has built in. The venue recently hosted a lengthy roster of A-list celebrities during the NBA Finals.

Andrea Davis, president and CEO of The Resiliency Initiative, has spent 25 years planning emergency response and crisis management for organizations including Disney, Walmart, the FIFA World Cup and Times Square's New Year's Eve celebration.

"Madison Square Garden is a venue known for their security protocol," Davis said. "It's top-notch. When I heard about it being an inside venue — and especially being at Madison — I thought, 'Oh, that just takes all of those security issues off the table.'"

Geoff Kohl, senior director of marketing for the Security Industry Association, agreed that Madison Square Garden's biggest advantage isn't simply the building itself — it's the systems already in place.

"It's got some built-in measures," Kohl said. "They're designed to control access very well. When you're dealing with an event like this, you want a venue where security isn't being invented for one night—it's something they do every day."

He pointed to discreet VIP entrances, controlled vendor access, multiple security checkpoints and a staff accustomed to hosting world leaders, championship events and A-list celebrities. Unlike a small venue assembling a security operation from scratch, Madison Square Garden already has experienced personnel, trusted vendors and established procedures for protecting high-profile guests.

That experience also extends behind the scenes. Kohl said venues and vendors that regularly work with celebrities are accustomed to strict nondisclosure agreements and limiting information to only those who need to know, reducing the risk of details leaking before an event.

Kohl said major events increasingly rely on advanced identity-verification systems, behavioral screening and other security technologies to validate guest lists. Similar biometric credentialing systems have been used at events such as the Super Bowl.

"Given a celebrity like Taylor, and even like Travis, their teams I'm sure are aware of known persons — stalkers, disruptors, bad actors," he said.

For Swift, that matters.

Over the past several years, the singer has spoken more openly than ever about what comes with living under constant public attention. During her Songwriters Hall of Fame induction speech in June, she reflected on "the complete loss of privacy" that accompanied her rise from teenage songwriter to global superstar.

Years earlier, she explored the same tension in "Peace," wondering whether she could ever give a partner an ordinary life.

In "Miss Americana," Scott Swift recalled buying armored vehicles because of concerns about threats against his daughter. After authorities thwarted an alleged terrorist plot targeting her Vienna Eras Tour concerts in 2024, Swift explained why she waited until after completing her London shows before publicly addressing the incident.

"My priority was finishing our European tour safely," she wrote, adding that she would not speak publicly if doing so risked provoking people who wanted to harm concertgoers.

USA TODAY has confirmed permits related to Madison Square Garden were filed for July 2 through July 4. However, those records do not identify Swift or Kelce and do not confirm the event is connected to the couple.

Then there's the added layer of public-records secrecy. In New York City, marriage applications and certificates are exempt from disclosure under the state's Freedom of Information Law, meaning citizens and reporters can't simply request a marriage license to verify wedding plans.

How Tennessee compares to New York for security

Although the Nashville area does have airports that cater to private jets, such as John C. Tune Airport and Smyrna Airport, and quiet estates celebrities can rent out completely for privacy, such as Cortina Farms in Leiper's Fork where country singer Cole Swindell got married, the Big Apple offers better traffic flow for an event with high-profile guests.

For a wedding of this magnitude, planning has to go beyond the venue itself.

When word spread that Swift attended Jack Antonoff and Margaret Qualley's 2023 wedding on the New Jersey shore, hundreds of fans and photographers descended on the quiet beach community. Streets became clogged as people waited outside venues hoping to catch a glimpse of the singer.

Davis said events of that scale require planning months — sometimes a year — in advance, with contingencies built into nearly every scenario.

Large-scale events involve tabletop exercises with law enforcement, fire departments, emergency managers and venue officials running through countless scenarios — evacuations, suspicious packages, drones and crowd surges.

Even if Madison Square Garden ultimately proves to be a decoy, using a public venue would also invite questions afterward about the resources devoted to the effort. If city agencies coordinated security, traffic planning or other logistics for an event that never took place there, critics could question whether public resources were unnecessarily tied up during one of New York's busiest holiday weekends.

Plus, New York has one more security advantage compared to Tennessee: marriage licenses in the Volunteer State are public records, while both New York and Rhode Island offer better privacy related to marriage licenses.

For the superstar whose private jet is tracked, whose every appearance is documented and whose movements become global headlines within minutes, an arena transformed into a private world may be the closest thing to privacy that money can buy.

Swift may follow a private ceremony with a public party

Money can create a romantic atmosphere anywhere, according to Valerie Edwards, a luxury wedding planner out of Big Sky, Montana.

"You can create a venue from scratch," Edwards said. "Make it your own, customize it, make it what Taylor and Travis want."

If Swift wanted, the basketball court could disappear beneath gardens, sailcloth tents, hydrangeas or even a recreation of one of her famous Rhode Island celebrations for Independence Day. Millions of dollars can transform hardwood into almost anything imaginable.

Concerts and award shows reinvent arenas every year through temporary architecture and production design. Although Edwards said to transform MSG into a wedding would take more than a week, Swift could treat it like the Eras Tour where the set was built and constructed for rehearsals at Rock Lititz in Pennsylvania. At Rock Lititz, Swift rehearsed before ever appearing at the first location in Arizona. Will 90 semi trucks — which is how many carried her full Eras Tour production across so many states — arrive at Madison Square before the alleged hold date of July 2?

"She doesn't necessarily do trendy things," Edwards said. "She's kind of a trendsetter in a lot of ways. So how could she have a wedding that's private, because obviously privacy is super important, but then also make it her own?"

Edwards also doesn't rule out a private ceremony elsewhere followed by a reception or concert inside the arena — a combination that would preserve the intimacy of exchanging vows while taking advantage of everything Madison Square Garden already offers, including the production capabilities for potential guests such as Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks or Ed Sheeran to perform. It would be like a Spotify playlist in real life.

"We're live music kind of people," Kelce recently said on "New Heights."

Swift echoed the sentiment during an appearance on "The Graham Norton Show," joking that if there were a stage, Ed Sheeran would inevitably find himself performing.

Even those believed to be on the guest list appear to know very little. Speaking at Tight End University, San Francisco 49ers star George Kittle said he already had his tuxedo picked out but suggested guests were intentionally being kept in the dark about the celebration's details — another indication of the tightly controlled planning surrounding the event.

Whether the celebration happens at Madison Square Garden, somewhere else or a combination of both, the planning itself reflects the challenge of staging a once-in-a-lifetime event for two people whose every move is watched.

But maybe that's the final irony of "The Lucky One." Madison Square Garden may represent the closest thing to a rose garden the superstar can find, because it's one of the few places capable of giving two of the world's most recognizable people a measure of privacy.

Don't miss any Taylor Swift news; sign up for the free, weekly newsletter This Swift Beat.

Follow Taylor Swift reporter Bryan West on Instagram, TikTok and X as @BryanWestTV.

The Fourth of July weekend is the platform for patriotic pride, and this year, Miss Americana will get the headlines, the photo spreads, the last word in her skillfully planned celebration of love. She'll also get to celebrate one of her favorite holidays, as documented through the years from her Rhode Island mansion, in the grandest way possible.

Swift is no fan of President Donald Trump, which she confirmed by her endorsement of Kamala Harris during the 2024 election, writing in a post that she was voting for a leader who projects “calm and not chaos.”

No one except for Swift and Kelce and their inner circle knows if the – again, purported – nuptials were inked for the same weekend as the 250th anniversary of America as a deliberate jab at the administration or if the dates just happened to coincide.

"Our officers are already working overtime for 4th of July festivities & NYC taxpayers should NOT be on the hook."

The city's police unions have decried conditions in which police are set to be placed with the World Cup, the Independence Day holiday weekend and now the reported wedding. Unions have complained over the city's delayed holiday pay as police work through the weekend.

Citing officers' exhaustion after already working overtime, Scott Munro, president of the Detectives' Endowment Association, told The New York Times that Swift and Kelce should "have their wedding in Pennsylvania or wherever she's from." – Eduardo Cuevas

Taylor Swift wedding dress code

The dress code will be black tie, with guests encouraged to wear top hats for men and long gloves for women, for a "magical" summer night, a source told USA TODAY.

But what will the bride walk down the aisle wearing? Many fans expect a big, perhaps weird, reveal.

Ed Sheeran, who is a possible performer at the wedding, was spotted in Malta, New York, (about a three-hour drive from New York City) at pizza restaurant BuonaSera on the Lake with musician Aaron Dessner, who is one of Swift's longtime collaborators. A fan shared a photo on X that appears to show Sabrina Carpenter at Emmett's on Grove in New York City.

It won't be a far commute for the New York Knicks' starting five Jalen Brunson, Josh Hart, Mikal Bridges, OG Anunoby and Karl-Anthony Towns, who scored invitations, according to Page Six. After all, Madison Square Garden is where they helped the Knicks win the franchise's first NBA championship in 53 years.

Knicks starting 5 gets invite to Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding at MSG, per report

Very few details about other guests have emerged. Suki Waterhouse, Selena Gomez, the Haim sisters, Zoë Kravitz, Emma Stone, Gigi Hadid, and Jack Antonoff are all likely attendees.

Wedding schedule reports emerge

search_result.title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce's wedding photos remain elusive. Here's why
search_result.text:
Updated July 6, 2026, 12:56 p.m. ET

Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are keeping a "privacy sign on the door" of their July 3 wedding.

The superstar couple tied the knot in a massive celebration at Madison Square Garden in New York City with a star-studded guest list to boot.

But despite the highly publicized nuptials, photos of the couple at their ceremony or reception have yet to be released.

The only glimpses into the event so far have come from tidbits shared by those who attended. From "Blank Space" themed napkins to emotional vows, all signs indicate that Swift and Kelce have no room for regrets.

So where are the photos of the bride and groom? Here's what we know.

Need a news break? Check out the all new PLAY hub with puzzles, games and more!

Why are there no photos of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce at their wedding ... yet

Despite marrying at one of the most popular arenas in the world, Swift and Kelce have managed to keep the most intimate details of their wedding – like photos from their ceremony – under wraps.

It's likely that none of it was accidental.

The newlyweds reportedly required guests to sign nondisclosure agreements, a type of legal document requiring confidentiality, when receiving their invitations.

Several guests have also indicated that phones were not allowed inside the arena, which may have also been in an attempt to avert leaks.

The heightened privacy could mean Swift and Kelce are waiting to share photos on their own terms, like they did in announcing their engagement on their social media pages last year.

They could also opt for a more elaborate showing, like sharing the photos in a magazine spread, as is common for many celebrity weddings. There were also signs posted around the venue indicating that filming would be taking place on-site, leading some fans to speculate whether a documentary or wedding film could be coming.

Seeing wedding photos of Taylor and Travis? It's probably AI.

Fakers gonna fake.

Fans may have spotted AI-generated images of the couple's wedding online in the days surrounding the event. But one guest said technology could not compare to the real thing.

Joseph Kahn, a director who worked with Swift on music videos for songs including "Bad Blood" and "Blank Space," shared a photo of him and his wife Lotte at the event on social media.

"I told Lotte many times it was like living in the internet, and AI would crack if it tried to render it," he wrote on Instagram.

Prior to the wedding, one AI-generated photo of the couple even caught the groom's attention.

On July 2, comedian Bert Kreischer shared an AI-generated photo of himself with Swift and Kelce dressed as a bride and groom on Instagram.

"The moment @jason.kelce called me over to the bar for a shot," Kreischer captioned the photo, to which Kelce commented four laughing emojis.

Guests reveal some details from inside MSG

Despite the tight security inside the venue, several guests have shared details on the event.

Stevie Nicks performed at the wedding, "Good Morning America" co-anchor Robin Roberts confirmed on the July 4 morning show. Paul McCartney also performed, a source confirmed to USA TODAY.

"It really was intimate," Roberts continued before George Stephanopoulos added, "As intimate as it could possibly be given it was Madison Square Garden."

"It's hard to imagine that a place that big and with such stars could feel so personal and so intimate," Stephanopoulos said, describing the setup as a "garden inside the Garden."

Roberts said among the attendees were Swift and Kelce's "neighbors and high school friends." They also revealed that the couple wrote their own vows from "little books."

AMC CEO Adam Aron wrote in a lengthy post on X that has since been deleted that the inside of the venue was adorned with flamingo pink couches and curtains as well as lush green scenery emulating a garden party.

What we know about Taylor Swift's dress, Travis Kelce's tux

Swift's representative confirmed in a statement that the singer and her NFL husband both wore outfits designed by Christian Dior.

The bride's dress and groom's tux were both designed by Jonathan Anderson, the creative director of Dior's women's, men's and haute couture collections, the statement said.

Anderson collaborated with both Swift and Kelce on their pieces, it added. Swift's gown marks Anderson's first couture wedding dress created for a globally recognized celebrity.

This story has been updated with additional information.

Melina Khan is a national trending reporter for USA TODAY. Keep up with her on X @melinakh and Instagram @bymelinakhan.

June 30, 2026 Updated July 2, 2026, 12:07 p.m. ET

Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding is shaping up to be one of the biggest events of the year – if not the decade or century.

Though the couple still haven't confirmed when or where they'll get married, rumors and reports have emerged with clues about their upcoming nuptials. Details about the couple's big day, purportedly in Madison Square Garden on July 3, have continued to come to light – from production equipment load-ins, public permits and venue schedules to comments from teammates, city officials and friends of the bride and groom.

While none of those details independently confirm the celebration, many clues point in the same direction: a trail that may soon have guests singing, "Welcome to New York."

When are Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce getting married?

The "I Know Places" singer and her fiancé have not confirmed a wedding date.

Need a news break? Check out the all new PLAY hub with puzzles, games and more!

The couple's nuptials reportedly will be a multi-day event; The New York Times suggested a smaller gathering of about 100 people will take place July 2, with a much bigger celebration on July 3.

The timing aligns with both of their schedules: Swift has long associated the holiday with gatherings of friends and family at her Rhode Island estate, while Chiefs training camp begins July 29. ESPN's Nate Taylor previously reported Kelce hoped to marry before camp opens.

Earlier speculation centered on June 13 after online rumors connected Swift to festivities near her Rhode Island home. Those reports were later debunked by a wedding planner involved, shifting attention to New York.

Eagle-eyed Swifties have also pointed to the schedules of the singer's inner circle. Actress Mariska Hargitay, who joined Swift courtside at Madison Square Garden during Game 4 of the NBA Finals, appears to have one notable gap in her Broadway schedule. During her run in "Every Brilliant Thing," July 3 is the only Friday without a scheduled performance.

Wedding venue: Is Taylor Swift really getting married at MSG?

Reports from TMZ and the New York Post have linked the couple's celebration to Madison Square Garden, though neither Swift nor Kelce has publicly commented.

USA TODAY has confirmed that permits related to Madison Square Garden were filed for July 2 through July 4. However, the records do not identify Swift or Kelce and do not reveal if the event is directly connected to the couple.

New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani referenced "Taylor Swift's wedding" while discussing public safety planning for the busy Fourth of July weekend. A city official later clarified that the mayor was referring to previously published media reports rather than confirming independent knowledge of the wedding.

The city does face a possible logjam of events, with World Cup games and celebrations for America's 250th anniversary of independence, a Sail250 maritime gathering – and a wedding that could attract hundreds of fans.

"Any one of those on their own would be a big deal in New York City," NYPD Commissioner Jessica Tisch said during a budget meeting. "All of them taken together are unprecedented and are going to place unusual, and I would say historic, demands on the New York City Police Department." Mamdani has agreed to overtime shifts as long as 12 hours for the NYPD.

Madison Square Garden executive chairman James Dolan has stayed mostly mum, neither confirming nor denying the wedding, during a WFAN interview. When one host suggested it would be the first wedding of its kind at the Garden, Dolan replied, "It's not a public event." (Sly Stone and Kathy Silva married at the venue in front of 21,000 fans, and the Unification Church had a mass wedding there in 1982.)

Madison Square Garden does hold personal significance for Swift. She headlined her first show there during the "Fearless" era in 2009, returned for the Speak Now Tour, performed at multiple Jingle Ball concerts and celebrated her 30th birthday at the venue in 2019. She was also courtside at the NBA Finals Game 4 in June.

And there is a notable gap in MSG's public calendar. The venue's website lists a June 28 concert by Rüfüs Du Sol and a July 7 performance by Bon Jovi, with no public events scheduled in between. But the venue has been known to go dark before on the long July 4th weekend.

Taylor Swift wedding guests: All the celebs on the guest list, so far

Some have compared the expected guest list to the Met Gala, with music, sports, Hollywood and even royalty expected to converge for what could become one of the most star-studded weddings.

Several of Kelce's teammates from the Kansas City Chiefs are expected to attend the nuptials.

The groom often talks about the team's quarterback, Patrick Mahomes, as family. Swift attended several Chiefs' games with Mahomes' wife, Brittany, and Kelce served as a groomsmen at their wedding.

Peters Clothiers, a Kansas City-based formalwear and tailoring company, shared a photo of Chiefs coach Andy Reid being fitted for formalwear, mentioning the team's general manager and VP of sports medicine in the caption: "KC CHIEFS Andy Reid, Brett Veach and Rick Burkholder getting ready for the Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift Wedding!"

While the couple haven't commented on the full guest list, Swift hinted Graham Norton would be among the guests. When Swift appeared on "The Graham Norton Show" in October to promote her album, "The Life of a Showgirl," they bantered over wedding timing and Norton's invitation.

She added that she had little interest in navigating the politics of a small guest list, explaining that larger weddings eliminate the need to decide which friends and acquaintances make the cut.

Suki Waterhouse, in an interview with Variety, said she'll be there.

"I'm gonna go to Taylor's wedding, and maybe I'll get some inspiration" for her own upcoming wedding to Robert Pattinson, she said. "It will be amazing."

Close friend and collaborator Ed Sheeran is an expected attendee. The "Shape of You" singer is touring, but doesn't have a performance scheduled on July 3; his next show is July 4 in Denver. On a U.K. radio show, she was asked if he'd perform at the wedding.

"Oh it would be hard to keep him from it, I think!" said the pop superstar.

San Francisco 49ers tight end George Kittle confirmed he'll be attending the wedding, saying he already has a blue suit picked out.

"I'm half expecting there to be a jumbo jet on a runway and they put us on a plane somewhere," Kittle said at Tight End University in Nashville, suggesting guests are still being kept in the dark about the location.

Several other close friends, including Abigail Anderson Berard, Ashley Avignone, and Este, Danielle and Alana Haim are expected to attend.

Even Prince William is hoping for an invite. During an interview on Heart FM, the future king said "No comment," when asked if he'd be there. "I'm hoping and I'm sure there might be an invitation around, but we'll see."

Who is designing Taylor Swift's wedding dress?

While Swift has not revealed who designed her gown – and there may be several – USA TODAY style reporter Morgan Evans has speculated about several possibilities based on designers she's frequently worn throughout her career.

Among the names drawing the most attention: Monse, the New York brand founded by Laura Kim and Fernando Garcia (Swift wore some of their looks when they served as co-creative directors at Oscar de la Renta); Stella McCartney (she's rumored to be attending the wedding); Jonathan Anderson of Christian Dior; Vivienne Westwood, known for romantic corseted bridal gowns; Ralph Lauren, whose striped dress Swift wore in her engagement announcement; Oscar de la Renta, a longtime red-carpet favorite; Jackson Wiederhoeft, whose designs Swift has recently embraced; and Givenchy under creative director Sarah Burton.

Who is planning the wedding?

Page Six linked luxury event planner Mark Seed to the celebration, a choice that would align with the couple's emphasis on privacy and discretion.

Seed maintains one of the lowest public profiles in the celebrity-event industry. His website contains little more than a logo and contact information. He has previously been connected to high-profile weddings involving Jennifer Lawrence, Jack Antonoff and Margaret Qualley.

Fans may remember Antonoff and Qualley's 2023 New Jersey wedding because Swift attended. After word spread that she was among the guests, crowds of fans and photographers inundated the community hoping to catch a glimpse of the singer.

What about gifts?

Kittle also revealed that – aside from secrecy – the couple has one wedding rule: "They said absolutely no gifts."

When will Swift and Kelce share all the details?

"Your English teacher and your gym teacher are getting married," Swift posted on Instagram when the couple announced their engagement – a full two weeks after Kelce popped the question.

Ever since they've been excited about the day, but understandably guarded. During a May 6 episode of "New Heights," golfer Rory McIlroy reflected on how memorable weddings can be.

Kelce's response was brief: "I can't wait."

search_result.title: Taylor Swift's wedding fits in her canon of uncool | Opinion
search_result.text:
July 3, 2026, 4:02 a.m. ET

“The wedding was charming, if a little gauche,” singer-songwriter Taylor Swift sang on “The Last Great American Dynasty,” a standout track from her seminal 2020 album, “Folklore.” What originated as Swift chronicling the legacy of her Rhode Island mansion (and the misogyny therein) now reads as some prescient self-mythologizing, as the pop juggernaut is set to marry football player Travis Kelce in another “wedding of the century.”

The wedding, slated for July 3 at Madison Square Garden (yes, the stadium in New York City), has caused much cultural fervor. Some for its bookending of the Kelce–Swift love story (that’s the “charming” part … I guess) but most for its grandiose display of tackiness (there goes that “gauche" part).

I understand those who lean toward the latter. Madison Square Garden certainly won’t impress those who’ve been building their aesthetic dream weddings on Pinterest since they were 13; nor is it reminiscent of the scenic French Riviera or Italian countryside, where celebrities tend to host their weddings.

Anyone who’s been to MSG knows it’s not exactly scenic, nor romantic, nor clean, even. To Swift and Kelce’s credit, though, it takes a certain type of loveblindess to find romance in such a place. That’s the thought that constantly orbited my head as I scratched it, trying to make sense of this choice in venue. Taylor Swift has never struck me as a woman of particular taste. Why should her wedding prove me otherwise?

Taylor Swift was never 'cool'

I mean, Swift’s whole artifice presents herself as the “every girl” – the band geek who prefers sneakers over high heels, the one who never fit in with the popular crowd or the it girls of the day. Her run-ins with those who do hold some cultural cache exposed just how corny and ham-fisted her attempts at cool can be.

Need a news break? Check out the all new PLAY hub with puzzles, games and more!

Take, for example, “Actually Romantic,” her supposed response to Charli xcx shade, or all of the “Reputation” era. Swift may have been valid in her responses then, and vindicated now with the Kanye nonsense, but it was tough to watch her do the whole “bad girl” thing.

Nevertheless, the biggest throughline of Swift’s career is her portrayal of the hopeless romantic. Alas, she’s accomplished “the thing,” she's willed her love story prophecy so, and it's culminated in this moment. Swift’s wedding won’t please internet tastemakers; her dress won’t impress fashion historians, but chicness was never her penchant.

Hosting a wedding event at MSG is Swift remaining faithful to her canon of uncool.

Let Tay have her tacky 'Taywedding'

This becomes abundantly clear when diving into the ridiculous details of this whole affair. Roads in Midtown Manhattan are scheduled to be closed, and hundreds of police officers are set to patrol the area for this two-day event, according to The New York Times. Approximately 1,000 guests are expected to attend, according to CNN.

At one point, there was a rumored castle being constructed inside Madison Square Garden. Male guests are encouraged to wear top hats for a "magical" summer night, a source told USA TODAY.

Taking it a step forward, as tenured Swifties know, it's in line with the references to storybook romances and fairytale endings sprinkled throughout her discography: "White Horse," "The Story of Us," "Enchanted," "Today Was a Fairytale," (how much more on-the-nose can you get?) "Castles Crumbling" and, most recently, "The Fate of Ophelia" just to name a few.

Just when I thought this couldn't get any tawdrier! Apparently, there was a mix-up, and workers had to rip up a newly placed red carpet for a purple one (?)

Most details regarding the wedding are kept top-secret to the dismay of some guests who would prefer a bit more clarity. A wedding celebration for one of the world’s most famous women in the center of the world's busiest city during the July Fourth weekend isn’t conducive to secrecy.

Perhaps, for such a high-profile couple, the best place to hide is in plain sight. MSG is covered by a roof, has no windows, and is known for having an excellent and disciplined security team.

Swift isn't one to get married in secret like, say, actress Zendaya, whose marital status with actor Tom Holland remained unknown for months. Swift's always been unabashed about her desire for matrimony. Remember the Instagram video of her singing "Champagne Problems" in a wedding gown?

With her MSG wedding, Swift embodies the "to be cringe is to be free" mantra adopted by many recently. Her desires are hers to have and to hold; they won't be stifled to fit a curated Vogue magazine spread.

So, dear reader, let her make those NFL players wear top hats. Let her fulfill the love story prophecy she designed. Let Taylor Swift have her tacky wedding!

Kofi Mframa is a columnist and digital producer for USA TODAY and the USA TODAY Network.

search_result.title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce wedding at Madison Square Garden? Pros ...
search_result.text:
June 11, 2026, 3:05 p.m. ET

A wedding at Madison Square Garden between Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce might be charming. Whether it would be "a little gauche" for "The Last Great American Dynasty" singer is still up for debate.

Reports from TMZ and the New York Post have fueled speculation that the couple could exchange vows inside the World's Most Famous Arena, though a representative for Swift did not respond to USA TODAY's request for comment, and neither Swift nor Kelce has publicly addressed the reports.

At first glance, the location seems far-fetched. Madison Square Garden is an arena. For decades, the venue that seats 20,000 people has been the place where sports, entertainment and American culture collide. It is home to the New York Knicks and New York Rangers. It hosted the Democratic National Convention in 1976, 1980 and 1992, along with the Republican National Convention in 2004. More than 15,000 people filled the arena in 1962 when Marilyn Monroe famously sang "Happy Birthday, Mr. President" to John F. Kennedy during a fundraising gala celebrating the president's 45th birthday.

If the reported wedding plans prove true, Swift and Kelce would simply be adding another chapter to the venue's overflowing larger-than-life history.

There is also precedent.

Need a news break? Check out the all new PLAY hub with puzzles, games and more!

In 1974, Sly Stone married actress Kathy Silva onstage at Madison Square Garden during a Sly and the Family Stone concert. While the circumstances were dramatically different from a private celebrity wedding, the event remains one of the most unusual ceremonies ever held inside the arena. In 1982, Madison Square Garden hosted a mass wedding ceremony involving more than 2,000 couples as part of a Unification Church Marriage Blessing.

Pros of a Madison Square Garden wedding: Privacy and personal history

The biggest argument in favor of Madison Square Garden, however, may not be its history, but its practicality.

Unlike a traditional wedding venue, MSG was designed to accommodate celebrities, athletes and major public figures. Beneath the arena sits a maze of private corridors, service tunnels, loading docks and restricted-access entrances. VIP guests can arrive and leave without ever interacting with the public. Security checkpoints, credential systems and crowd-management infrastructure are already built into the venue.

For a couple whose every public appearance generates international headlines, privacy may be worth almost as much as symbolism.

And while most people think of the main arena bowl, Madison Square Garden is far more than a basketball court surrounded by seats. The complex includes numerous lounges, clubs, suites and event spaces that can host gatherings ranging from a few dozen guests to several hundred. Some spaces span roughly 1,400 square feet, while larger hospitality areas exceed 5,000 square feet, according to the complex's website. The venue can accommodate intimate receptions just as easily as large-scale productions say if Paul McCartney or Stevie Nicks wanted to sing the couple a song.

Then there is Swift's personal connection to the building.

Long before she was breaking attendance records around the world on the Eras Tour, Madison Square Garden played a role in her rise to superstardom. She headlined her first Garden show during the "Fearless" era in 2009. She returned for "Speak Now" performances, appeared during multiple Jingle Ball concerts and celebrated her 30th birthday there in 2019. Most recently, Swift returned to the Garden as a fan rather than a performer, attending Game 4 of the NBA Finals on June 10 alongside sisters Este and Alana Haim as the New York Knicks hosted the San Antonio Spurs.

The arena is also tied to a larger chapter of her life: New York itself.

The city became the backdrop for one of the most significant reinventions of Swift's career. It inspired 2014's "1989," the album that marked her transition from country superstar to global pop icon. If Nashville built Taylor Swift, New York redefined her.

A Madison Square Garden wedding would carry that symbolism, representing another new beginning in a city already associated with some of the biggest turning points of her life.

Cons of a Madison Square Garden wedding: Is MSG actually romantic?

Still, there are drawbacks.

For all its history and prestige, Madison Square Garden remains an arena. Creating warmth and intimacy inside a venue built for nearly 20,000 people would require extraordinary effort and cost. There are no gardens. No waterfront views. No sweeping estate grounds. Every romantic detail would need to be carefully designed and constructed.

The venue's concessions stands would need to disappear behind draping and custom installations. Thousands of seats could be curtained off or incorporated into the event itself. Hallways designed for hockey fans buying hot dogs and beer would need to feel appropriate for guests arriving in tuxedos and designer gowns. Even something as basic as directing wedding guests to arena restrooms rather than traditional ballroom facilities underscores the challenge of transforming a sports venue into an elegant celebration.

There is also the question of perception. A wedding at Madison Square Garden would undoubtedly become a cultural event. Critics would see it as an excessive spectacle. Supporters would find it a uniquely fitting tribute to two people whose careers sit at the center of sports and entertainment.

Don't miss any Taylor Swift news; sign up for the free, weekly newsletter This Swift Beat.

Follow Taylor Swift reporter Bryan West on Instagram, TikTok and X as @BryanWestTV.

search_result.title: A Swift-Kelce wedding on center court? Madison Square Garden ...
search_result.text:
Updated June 25, 2026, 5:18 p.m. ET

The main arena at Madison Square Garden can be configured for events like banquets or cocktail receptions.

In addition to the main arena, the venue offers smaller spaces like a theater and various VIP spaces.

Can you imagine holding your wedding at a 19,000-seat arena?

Of course, the average person can't fathom this. But Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce, who are rumored to be holding a pretty big celebration at Madison Square Garden in July, aren't average. According to Forbes, Swift's net worth is $2 billion. Kelce hasn't done too shabby either, racking in $111 million from NFL contracts alone.

With seemingly endless resources, the two could probably turn any location into a wedding venue. And according to its website, Madison Square Garden can host more than just a concert or sporting event.

Here's a look at the different parts of the World's Most Famous Arena that might be utilized by the engaged couple should they decide to hold their wedding in the midtown Manhattan venue. As of press time, a representative for Madison Square Garden didn't respond to USA TODAY's request for comment.

The main arena: Will Taylor and Travis wed on a basketball court?

The biggest part of Madison Square Garden is the most known: a 19,000-seat bowl covered with an iconic roof. The floor houses a basketball court for the NBA's Knicks and an ice rink for the NHL's Rangers. The Garden also regularly hosts college sports, including a portion of the St. John's men's basketball team's home schedule.

Need a news break? Check out the all new PLAY hub with puzzles, games and more!

But the arena can be configured for much more than concerts and sporting events. According to the venue's website, there are at least three other layout options for the main court: a cocktail reception (2,000 people), banquet event with seated tables, catering area and dance floor (1,250), and theater-style seating in rows (2,000).

The Garden's main floor has hosted dinners as recently as this year. In April, Chase Sapphire Reserve held a dinner on the Knicks famed court. Invited guests included actor Ben Stiller and former Knick stars Walt "Clyde" Frazier and Stephon Marbury. Popular restaurants San Sabino and Don Angie catered the event.

Could Taylor and Travis use a different part of Madison Square Garden?

While the arena is the largest option for hosting a party inside Madison Square Garden, it is not the only option. MSG also houses a 5,500 seat theater. And there are multiple club and VIP rooms that can be rented.

The largest of the VIP areas is the Delta Sky360 club, which is typically accessible to attendees with floor tickets at Garden events, and can hold 300 people for cocktails and 150 for banquet or theater seating. The room is already set up with a large bar and catering capabilities in addition to private bathrooms and elevator access. This area could serve as a space for cocktail hour or as a welcome spot for guests en route to other areas of the arena.

There's also the Chase Lounge, which is typically open before Knicks and Rangers games exclusively for Chase card holders. The space fits 250 and is already set up for both dining and cocktails. The room also features a 60-person board room.

The Lexus Terrace, which overlooks the main entrance of the Garden that faces 7th Avenue, is also available for rent, with capacity for 200 people.

Where were past MSG weddings held?

While a wedding inside Madison Square Garden is unusual, it is not unprecedented. In 1974, Sly Stone married actress Kathy Silva onstage at Madison Square Garden during a Sly and the Family Stone concert. In 1982, the venue hosted a mass wedding ceremony involving more than 2,000 couples as part of a Unification Church Marriage Blessing.

Both of those weddings occurred in the main arena. But neither involved arguably the most famous person in the world.

Contributing: Bryan West, USA TODAY

The timing aligns with both of their schedules. Swift has long associated the holiday with gatherings of friends and family at her Rhode Island estate, while Chiefs training camp begins July 29. ESPN's Nate Taylor previously reported Kelce hoped to marry before camp opens.

Need a news break? Check out the all new PLAY hub with puzzles, games and more!

Earlier speculation centered on June 13 after online rumors connected Swift to festivities near her Rhode Island home. Those reports were later debunked by a wedding planner involved, shifting attention to New York.

Eagle-eyed Swifties have also pointed to the schedules of the singer's inner circle. For example, actress Mariska Hargitay, who joined Swift courtside at Madison Square Garden during Game 4 of the NBA Finals, appears to have one notable gap in her Broadway schedule. During her run in "Every Brilliant Thing," July 3 is the only Friday without a scheduled performance.

Where will the wedding be?

Reports from TMZ and the New York Post have linked the couple's celebration to Madison Square Garden, though neither Swift nor Kelce has publicly commented.

USA TODAY has confirmed that permits related to Madison Square Garden were filed for July 2 through July 4. However, the records do not identify Swift or Kelce and do not confirm the event is connected to the couple.

The rumors picked up steam after New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani referenced "Taylor Swift's wedding" while discussing public safety planning for the busy Fourth of July weekend. A city official later clarified that the mayor was referring to previously published media reports rather than confirming independent knowledge of the event.

Madison Square Garden executive chairman James Dolan was asked about the wedding during a WFAN interview. He stayed mostly mum while the hosts talked, never confirming nor denying the wedding. When one host suggested it would be the first wedding of its kind at the Garden, Dolan replied, "It's not a public event." (Sly Stone and Kathy Silva married at the venue in front of 21,000 fans, and the Unification Church had a mass wedding there in 1982.)

Madison Square Garden does hold personal significance for Swift. She headlined her first show there during the "Fearless" era in 2009, returned for the "Speak Now" Tour, performed at multiple Jingle Ball concerts there and celebrated her 30th birthday at the venue in 2019. She was also courtside at the NBA Finals Game 4 in June.

And there is a notable gap in MSG's public calendar. The venue's website lists a June 28 concert by Rüfüs Du Sol and a July 7 performance by Bon Jovi, with no public events scheduled in between. But the venue has been known to go dark before on the long July 4th weekend.

Who are the guests at Taylor and Travis' wedding?

Several of Kelce's teammates from the Kansas City Chiefs are expected to attend the nuptials. The groom often talks about the team's quarterback, Patrick Mahomes, as family. ("I'm playing against a brother and I'm playing with one," he said ahead of the Super Bowl when they faced brother Jason's Philadelphia Eagles.) Swift attended several Chiefs' games with Mahomes' wife, Brittany. Kelce served as a groomsmen at their wedding.

Peters Clothiers, a Kansas City-based formalwear and tailoring company, shared a photo of Chiefs coach Andy Reid being fitted for formalwear, mentioning the team's general manager and VP of sports medicine in the caption: "KC CHIEFS Andy Reid, Brett Veach and Rick Burkholder getting ready for the Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift Wedding!"

While the couple hasn't commented on the full guest list, Swift hinted Graham Norton would be among the guests. When Swift, in October 2025, appeared on "The Graham Norton Show" to promote her album, "The Life of a Showgirl," the host asked if she was getting married in 2026.

She responded, "Oh you’ll know."

"So you’re going big?" he followed up.

"Meaning I was going to invite you to it," she said.

"If you’re inviting me, it’s very big," Norton replied.

"It's huge," Swift said. She added that she had little interest in navigating the politics of a small guest list, explaining that larger weddings eliminate the need to decide which friends and acquaintances make the cut.

Suki Waterhouse, in an interview with Variety, said she'll be there.

"Nothing scarier than the actual planning part, right?" she said, as she's in the midst of planning her own ceremony with Robert Pattinson. "I'm gonna go to Taylor’s wedding, and maybe I’ll get some inspiration. It will be amazing."

Close friend and collaborator Ed Sheeran is an expected attendee. The "Shape of You" singer is touring, but doesn't have a performance scheduled on July 3; his next show is July 4 in Denver. On a UK radio show, she was asked if he'd perform at the wedding.

"Oh it would be hard to keep him from it, I think!" said the pop superstar. "That's the thing, he's like, 'I'm always being asked to sing at weddings.' It's like, 'Ed, if there is a stage, you know you’ll be on it.' He knows what people want, and he wants to give people what they want."

San Francisco 49ers tight end George Kittle confirmed he'll be attending the wedding, saying he already has a blue suit picked out.

"I'm half expecting there to be a jumbo jet on a runway and they put us on a plane somewhere," Kittle said at Tight End University in Nashville, suggesting guests are still being kept in the dark about the location. He added that when he asked Kelce whether the celebration was at Madison Square Garden, "he laughed at me."

Several other close friends, including Abigail Anderson Berard, Ashley Avignone, and Este, Danielle and Alana Haim are expected to attend.

Even Prince William is hoping for an invite. During an interview on Heart FM, the future king said "No comment," when asked if he'd be there. "I'm hoping and I'm sure there might be an invitation around, but we'll see."

Who is designing Taylor's wedding dress?

While Swift has not revealed who designed her gown—and there may be several—USA TODAY fashion expert Morgan Evans has speculated about several possibilities based on designers she's frequently worn throughout her career.
